source.title: Add weighted probe sampler and autogen performance hint annotations
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.body:
Summary: Introduces a weighted probe-sampling utility and an optional 'performance_hints' metadata annotation for model profiles. The sampler produces small, representative workload slices used by the registry to auto-generate suggested batching/quantization profiles and to estimate cost/latency impact for release notes.
Motivation: onboarding new models currently relies on heavy full-workload profiling or manual hinting. This change reduces friction by (1) providing a lightweight sampled footprint for quick calibrations, and (2) surfacing automated performance hints that can be included in changelogs and the model catalog.
Context and linked work: implements parts of ENG-1428 (deploy-time perf hint generation) and ties back to SUP-7241 (customer report: slow cold-starts for large decoder-only models).
Design notes:
- Weighted sampling: we sample examples from a model's telemetry or synthetic probe corpus with weights derived from token-length distribution, presence of long-context markers, and a novelty score (n-gram surprise). The output is a compact 'probe-seed' tarball (gzip jsonl) that the onboarding pipeline consumes.
- Performance hints: a small metadata map added to profile.yaml containing keys: cpu_estimate_ms, gpu_estimate_ms, warm_kv_cache_recommendation, suggested_batch_sizes, quantization_safety. Hints are intentionally conservative and accompanied by provenance attributes.
- Changelog automation: onboarding pipeline now offers an optional step to generate a short 'perf blurb' based on the hints. This blurb is attached to the release draft and can be edited by maintainers.
Checklist:
- [x] Add weighted sampler implementation and unit tests
- [x] Add profile annotation schema and schema migration path
- [x] Integrate sampler output into onboarding pipeline (dry-run)
- [x] Add small e2e that exercises perf blurb generation
- [x] Update registry docs and example onboarding snippet
- [x] Manual QA on 3 model families (decoder-only, encoder-decoder, embed-only)
Commits (summary):
- feat(sampler): introduce WeightedProbeSampler + sampling heuristics
- feat(onboard): consume probe-seed and emit performance_hints in profile.yaml
- chore(schema): bump profile schema v2.4 and add migration script
- test(e2e): add lightweight e2e for perf blurb generation
- docs: add onboarding example and admin guide
Notes on safety and accuracy: performance_hints are advisory; precise latency/cost remains the responsibility of the serving runtime. Hints include a provenance tag (sampler: v1, telemetry_window: 72h) and a confidence score.
Migration strategy: profile schema bumped to 2.4. Existing profiles without hints are left unchanged; the on-demand migration tool will produce probe-seeds for historical models when requested.
Files changed (high level):
- registry/sampler/weighted_sampler.py (core algorithm, 220 lines)
- registry/sampler/heuristics.py (scoring and weighting helpers)
- registry/onboard/consume_probe.py (ingest probe-seed and merge hints)
- registry/schema/profile_v2_4.yaml (schema diff + migration notes)
- tests/sampler/test_weighted_sampler.py (unit tests)
- tests/onboard/test_perf_blurb.py (integration smoke tests)
- docs/onboarding/perf-hints.md (docs and sample release blurb)
CI: initial run contained a flaky e2e on arm runners; fixed by adding a per-run seed and re-running tests. All checks green on final run.
Backwards compatibility: no breaking changes to existing APIs; schema migration is additive.
Release notes: see "release_notes" field.
Related PRs: reference for design review: pr-214030 (hardware-affinity sharding) and pr-51782 (autodraft impact statements)
Review conversation (high level):
- Diego: asked for clarification on the novelty scoring and requested a threshold to avoid sampling only long outliers. Response: added an adjustable clip parameter with sane default (0.95 percentile) and unit tests.
- Marta: requested extra provenance metadata; response: added telemetry_window and sampler_version fields and documented them.
- Leo: asked for guardrails on perf blurb wording to avoid committing inaccurate claims; response: blurb generation is templated and includes confidence qualifiers; maintainers can edit draft before publishing.
Merge: squash merge to main after approvals and passing CI.
commits: feat(sampler): introduce WeightedProbeSampler + sampling heuristics
feat(onboard): consume probe-seed and emit performance_hints in profile.yaml
chore(schema): bump profile schema v2.4 and add migration script
test(e2e): add lightweight e2e for perf blurb generation
docs: add onboarding example and admin guide
review_comments: Diego Alvarez: Please add a clip/threshold so we don't overweight rare 8k+ prompts.
Priya Natarajan: Added clip param defaulting to 0.95 percentile, updated tests.
Marta Chen: Add telemetry_window and sampler_version to provenance metadata.
Priya Natarajan: Added metadata and documented the fields in schema.
Leo Fischer: Ensure perf blurb language is conservative and editable.
Priya Natarajan: Blurb output templated with confidence tags, maintainers can edit before publish.
source.merge_details: Squash merged by Marta Chen on 2025-11-14. Merge commit: 7a9c3bf. All CI checks green. No release blocker labels.
source.release_notes:
- Added WeightedProbeSampler to generate compact probe-seeds for onboarding.
- Added optional performance_hints in profile.yaml (cpu_estimate_ms, gpu_estimate_ms, warm_kv_cache_recommendation, suggested_batch_sizes, quantization_safety).
- Schema bumped to v2.4; migration tool included.
- Onboarding pipeline can now auto-draft a perf blurb for release notes; maintainers should review before publishing.

Migration strategy: profile schema bumped to 2.4. Existing profiles without hints are left unchanged; the on-demand migration tool will produce probe-seeds for historical models when requested.

text:
Summary: Introduces a weighted probe-sampling utility and an optional 'performance_hints' metadata annotation for model profiles. The sampler produces small, representative workload slices used by the registry to auto-generate suggested batching/quantization profiles and to estimate cost/latency impact for release notes.
Motivation: onboarding new models currently relies on heavy full-workload profiling or manual hinting. This change reduces friction by (1) providing a lightweight sampled footprint for quick calibrations, and (2) surfacing automated performance hints that can be included in changelogs and the model catalog.
Context and linked work: implements parts of ENG-1428 (deploy-time perf hint generation) and ties back to SUP-7241 (customer report: slow cold-starts for large decoder-only models).
Design notes:
- Weighted sampling: we sample examples from a model's telemetry or synthetic probe corpus with weights derived from token-length distribution, presence of long-context markers, and a novelty score (n-gram surprise). The output is a compact 'probe-seed' tarball (gzip jsonl) that the onboarding pipeline consumes.
- Performance hints: a small metadata map added to profile.yaml containing keys: cpu_estimate_ms, gpu_estimate_ms, warm_kv_cache_recommendation, suggested_batch_sizes, quantization_safety. Hints are intentionally conservative and accompanied by provenance attributes.
- Changelog automation: onboarding pipeline now offers an optional step to generate a short 'perf blurb' based on the hints. This blurb is attached to the release draft and can be edited by maintainers.
Checklist:
- [x] Add weighted sampler implementation and unit tests
- [x] Add profile annotation schema and schema migration path
- [x] Integrate sampler output into onboarding pipeline (dry-run)
- [x] Add small e2e that exercises perf blurb generation
- [x] Update registry docs and example onboarding snippet
- [x] Manual QA on 3 model families (decoder-only, encoder-decoder, embed-only)
Commits (summary):
- feat(sampler): introduce WeightedProbeSampler + sampling heuristics
- feat(onboard): consume probe-seed and emit performance_hints in profile.yaml
- chore(schema): bump profile schema v2.4 and add migration script
- test(e2e): add lightweight e2e for perf blurb generation
- docs: add onboarding example and admin guide
Notes on safety and accuracy: performance_hints are advisory; precise latency/cost remains the responsibility of the serving runtime. Hints include a provenance tag (sampler: v1, telemetry_window: 72h) and a confidence score.
Migration strategy: profile schema bumped to 2.4. Existing profiles without hints are left unchanged; the on-demand migration tool will produce probe-seeds for historical models when requested.
Files changed (high level):
- registry/sampler/weighted_sampler.py (core algorithm, 220 lines)
- registry/sampler/heuristics.py (scoring and weighting helpers)
- registry/onboard/consume_probe.py (ingest probe-seed and merge hints)
- registry/schema/profile_v2_4.yaml (schema diff + migration notes)
- tests/sampler/test_weighted_sampler.py (unit tests)
- tests/onboard/test_perf_blurb.py (integration smoke tests)
- docs/onboarding/perf-hints.md (docs and sample release blurb)
CI: initial run contained a flaky e2e on arm runners; fixed by adding a per-run seed and re-running tests. All checks green on final run.
Backwards compatibility: no breaking changes to existing APIs; schema migration is additive.
Release notes: see "release_notes" field.
Related PRs: reference for design review: pr-214030 (hardware-affinity sharding) and pr-51782 (autodraft impact statements)

- Diego: asked for clarification on the novelty scoring and requested a threshold to avoid sampling only long outliers. Response: added an adjustable clip parameter with sane default (0.95 percentile) and unit tests.
- Marta: requested extra provenance metadata; response: added telemetry_window and sampler_version fields and documented them.
- Leo: asked for guardrails on perf blurb wording to avoid committing inaccurate claims; response: blurb generation is templated and includes confidence qualifiers; maintainers can edit draft before publishing.

source.title: Version claim notarizer and micro-benchmark replay harness
source.author: Priya Desai
source.description:
Problem: Onboarding teams were seeing transient mismatches between declared model version metadata and the performance stamps we publish. That led to confusion when a variant rolled with differing microbenchmark characteristics, which in turn caused rollout regressions. This PR introduces two complementary subsystems: a version-claim notarizer that enforces deterministic, signed version claims for model uploads and a micro-benchmark replay harness that deterministically replays a curated micro-benchmark suite to generate reproducible performance stamps.

Design summary: 1) Version claim notarizer: a lightweight preflight hook that validates incoming model metadata, computes a canonical claim (content hash + schema-normalized metadata), attaches a notarization token, and writes a provenance entry to the registry ledger. The notarizer supports optional KMS signing for enterprise Private installs. 2) Micro-benchmark replay harness: a small, deterministic runner that executes a compressed suite of microbenchmarks (latency hot paths, KV cache warmup, small/long context runs) in a sandboxed, seeded environment and outputs a compact performance stamp. The harness can be run as part of CI, on intake workers, or manually by release engineers.

What changed (high level): - New API endpoints: POST /v1/models/:id/claim and POST /v1/models/:id/replay-bench - Registry schema extension: model_provenance.claim_token and model_provenance.perf_stamp_v2 - New CLI subcommands: redwood-registry claim-notarize, redwood-registry bench-replay - Worker job: bench-replay-worker to schedule deterministic microbench runs and persist stamps - KMS integration hook under config/kms_signer (optional)

Commits (summary): [ci] add bench replay CI job; scaffold harness runner; schema: add perf_stamp_v2 and claim_token; implement notarizer preflight validation; add KMS signer interface + tests; integrate worker queue and publish hook; docs: add notarizer and bench-replay guide; tests: coverage for canonicalization and deterministic replay

Files changed (high level): src/registry/api/claims.py, src/registry/workers/bench_replay.py, src/registry/models/provenance.sql, tools/benchmarks/micro_suite/*.py, cli/redwood_registry/claim.py, docs/registry/notarizer.md, tests/test_claims.py, tests/test_bench_replay.py

Compatibility and rollout: The schema change is additive (perf_stamp_v2) and we default to falling back to the previous perf_stamp when absent. Notarizer is opt-in for existing ingestion flows; we enable it for the staged intake pipeline immediately behind a feature flag. KMS signing is gated behind config and not enabled in hosted by default.

Testing: Unit tests for canonicalization, round-trip signature verification, and deterministic benchmark outputs. End-to-end smoke: ingest a synthetic model tarball, notarize claim, enqueue bench replay, and assert persisted perf stamp. CI run required a fixed docker image with deterministic RNG seeds.

Why this is different: Previous PRs focused on release note generation or sample-based profiling. This change ties provenance (signed version claims) directly to a deterministic microbenchmark snapshot, enabling reliable compatibility assertions and automated, auditable changelog snippets derived from numeric perf diffs.

Rollout plan: 1) Enable feature flag for intake-staging; 2) Run bench-replay on historical models to backfill perf_stamp_v2 for a subset; 3) Monitor perf drift alerts and adjust micro-suite; 4) Gradually enable notarizer as the default for new uploads after 2 weeks of staging validation.
commits: a1b2c3c: scaffold bench replay runner and CI job
d4e5f6a: add schema fields perf_stamp_v2 and claim_token
b7c8d9e: implement claim canonicalization and notarizer preflight
f0e1d2c: add KMS signer interface and integration test
a9b8c7d: worker queue integration and persistence hooks
c6b5a4f: docs and CLI commands for claim-notarize and bench-replay
source.diff_snippet:
@@ src/registry/models/provenance.sql
+ALTER TABLE model_provenance ADD COLUMN claim_token TEXT;
+ALTER TABLE model_provenance ADD COLUMN perf_stamp_v2 JSONB;
@@ src/registry/api/claims.py
+def compute_canonical_claim(metadata, blobs):
+    # stable-serialize -> hash -> optional KMS sign
+    payload = canonical_serialize(metadata, blobs)
+    return sha256(payload).hexdigest()
review_thread: Ethan: Please ensure canonicalization is stable across python versions -- suggest using msgpack-canonical with explicit sortings.
Author(Priya): Switched to canonical_msgpack and added version pin in docker image. Added tests across 3 py versions.
Sofia: KMS signer interface looks good, but add a noop path for hosted. Also prefer async worker enqueue to avoid blocking intake.
Author(Priya): Added noop signer config and converted enqueue to background task. Updated docs accordingly.
Noah: Minor nits on CLI help text; approvable after fixes.
Author(Priya): Fixed CLI help text, rebased, and squashed commits.
Sofia: Approved.
Ethan: Approved.
Noah: Approved.
source.release_notes: Add: version claim notarizer and deterministic micro-benchmark replay harness. These provide signed provenance tokens and reproducible perf stamps for model onboarding. Backfill tooling and CLI added. Opt-in behind feature flag.
source.merge_outcome: Merged after two approvals and one round of requested changes addressing canonicalization and signer noop path. Backfill scheduled for next sprint.

source.title: Introduce declarative release annotations and runtime profile hinting
source.author: Ava Chen
source.release_notes: Add support for declarative release annotations that capture runtime hints (preferred quantization, expected 99th latency, recommended batching) and enhance changelog automation to include annotated release summaries. Migration: existing model manifests will be backfilled with default hint values via a provided migration script.
source.description:
Context and motivation:
We frequently onboard new models with performance guidance buried in PR bodies or internal wiki pages. That makes it hard for the serving runtime and release automation to pick optimal defaults (quantization, batch sizing, routing policy) and for release notes tools to produce consistent changelogs. This PR introduces a small, intentionally-declarative annotation layer on model release manifests and ties those annotations into the changelog generator and onboarding checks.

What this PR does (high-level):
1) Adds a new optional top-level 'release_annotations' block to model manifest schema. The block is expressive but constrained: keys include "recommended_quant", "latency_ms_p99", "preferred_batching" (list), "compat_notes", and "release_reason".
2) Implements a parser and validator (registry/annotations/validator.py) that enforces allowed keys, ranges (e.g., latency_ms_p99 > 0), and cross-field constraints (e.g., preferred_batching values must be one of ["single", "small", "large"]).
3) Extends the changelog assembler to consume release_annotations and produce a structured release blurb that highlights runtime hints and a concise change summary. The assembler now emits a short 'runtime_hints' section in the generated changelog JSON and prose.
4) Adds a migration utility (tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py) that scans existing manifests and emits a report + patch bundle with conservative default hints derived from existing performance profiles when available.
5) Connects annotations to the release gating pipeline: models with annotations that declare latency_ms_p99 will have additional automated smoke tests configured during CI to validate expected 95/99 latency under a representative micro-benchmark.
6) Adds docs and examples (docs/onboard/release-annotations.md) and CLI support (cli/modelctl annotate-release --file ...) for maintainers to add or update annotations.

Design notes and tradeoffs:
- Annotations are optional and backwards compatible. No manifest without annotations will be rejected. The migration tool encourages progressive adoption.
- Values are intentionally conservative; e.g., default recommended_quant is 'q4_0' where no fine-grained profiling exists. Teams with tighter SLOs can override.
- We avoid trying to express every runtime knob in the manifest to keep the contract stable. The annotation surface focuses on small, high-value hints that both serving and release automation can consume.

Commits (summary):
- 7f2d9b2: add schema extension and parser for release_annotations
- 02b4e6c: implement validator and unit tests for constraints and error messages
- 3a9d4c1: integrate annotations into changelog assembler and prose formatter
- bc12fda: migration tool to backfill annotations from existing profiles; dry-run mode
- f0e1a77: add CLI commands and docs; examples and e2e smoke tests
- e8c3b44: hook CI smoke test step to require latency check if latency_ms_p99 declared

Changed files (high level):
- registry/schema/model_manifest_v2.yaml (schema change)
- registry/annotations/validator.py (new)
- registry/annotations/parser.py (new)
- tooling/changelog/assembler.py (update to consume annotations)
- tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py (new migration)
- cli/modelctl/commands.py (annotate subcommand)
- tests/annotations/test_validator.py (unit tests)
- tests/e2e/test_release_annotation_smoke.py (CI smoke test harness)
- docs/onboard/release-annotations.md (docs)
- examples/onboard/sample-model-with-annotations.yaml (example manifest)

Review conversation (high-level):
- Marcus Patel (review): "Like the direction. Two asks: (1) ensure validator failure messages are actionable (line+example), (2) add a note about how runtime will treat unknown keys."
- Ava Chen (author): "Addressed: validator messages now include manifest path/line where available and validator ignores unknown keys with a warning (configurable). Updated tests to exercise unknown-key behavior."
- Lucia García (review): "Changelog output looks good; can we make the prose a single sentence if the runtime_hints set is small? Also, add a migration dry-run report format so SRE can audit changes before apply."
- Ava Chen: "Prose single-sentence collapse implemented; migration tool now emits dry-run JSON report and a human-readable patch summary. Added docs link in PR body."
- Daniel Kim (approve): "Verified e2e smoke test passes on my local run and CI. Approving."

CI and checks:
- Unit tests: pass (all annotations tests green)
- Lint: pass
- Changelog assembler integration test: pass
- E2E smoke (latency check): pass on CI; note: two flaky runs previously due to infra noise, resolved by pinning micro-benchmark seed and increasing sampling window.

Migration and rollout plan:
- Step 1 (dry-run): run tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py --dry-run across the registry, produce a review bundle and post to Slack channel #model-onboard for sign-off.
- Step 2 (staged apply): apply patches for low-risk models (models with existing perf profiles) and validate with CI smoke tests.
- Step 3 (full apply): after one week of signals and follow-ups, apply remaining conservative defaults and notify teams to refine annotations.

Follow-ups and TODOs (tracked as small follow-up tickets):
- Add an API endpoint for the console to render runtime_hints on model pages. (ENG-4794)
- Explore auto-suggesting preferred_batching based on historical traffic patterns.
- Consider mapping release annotations to automatic routing policies for Dedicated customers in a follow-up PR.

Why this is different from prior work:
Several prior PRs touched changelog assembly or profile stamping; this change centralizes runtime-relevant hints in a compact, auditable manifest extension and ties those hints directly to both release blurb generation and CI smoke tests. It reduces ad-hoc documentation and makes serving/runtime choices more discoverable and programmatic.

QA checklist (in PR body):
- [x] Schema changes validated with unit tests
- [x] Backfill migration dry-run produces expected patches
- [x] Changelog prose includes runtime_hints when present
- [x] E2E smoke latency checks pass when latency_ms_p99 declared
- [x] Docs updated and example manifest added

Merge outcome:
Merged by squash on 2025-11-12 after approvals and green CI. Rollout started with a dry-run and first staged apply to models with existing performance profiles.
source.commits: 7 commits (7f2d9b2, 02b4e6c, 3a9d4c1, bc12fda, f0e1a77, e8c3b44, 5d1aa33) - parser/validator, changelog integration, migration, CLI, tests
source.changed_files: registry/schema/model_manifest_v2.yaml, registry/annotations/parser.py, registry/annotations/validator.py, tooling/changelog/assembler.py, tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py, cli/modelctl/commands.py, docs/onboard/release-annotations.md, examples/onboard/sample-model-with-annotations.yaml, tests/annotations/test_validator.py, tests/e2e/test_release_annotation_smoke.py, .github/workflows/annotations-smoke.yml, README.md
source.notes_for_release_engineers: Dry-run required before apply. For models with latency hints, CI will run additional micro-benchmarks; be prepared for transient failures in noisy infra. Migration script provides --reject-list to skip models owned by security-sensitive teams.
source.audit_trail: migration dry-run produced patchset 2025-11-07-annotations-backfill.tar.gz; applied staged patchset on 2025-11-10 to 27 models; no regressions found during 72h observation window.
source.observability_impacts: Changelog assembler emits runtime_hints tag into release metadata which is ingested by the console; add dashboard card to surface annotation drift (ENG-4794).

- Step 2 (staged apply): apply patches for low-risk models (models with existing perf profiles) and validate with CI smoke tests.
- Step 3 (full apply): after one week of signals and follow-ups, apply remaining conservative defaults and notify teams to refine annotations.

Follow-ups and TODOs (tracked as small follow-up tickets):
- Add an API endpoint for the console to render runtime_hints on model pages. (ENG-4794)
- Explore auto-suggesting preferred_batching based on historical traffic patterns.
- Consider mapping release annotations to automatic routing policies for Dedicated customers in a follow-up PR.

Why this is different from prior work:
Several prior PRs touched changelog assembly or profile stamping; this change centralizes runtime-relevant hints in a compact, auditable manifest extension and ties those hints directly to both release blurb generation and CI smoke tests. It reduces ad-hoc documentation and makes serving/runtime choices more discoverable and programmatic.

QA checklist (in PR body):
- [x] Schema changes validated with unit tests
- [x] Backfill migration dry-run produces expected patches
- [x] Changelog prose includes runtime_hints when present
- [x] E2E smoke latency checks pass when latency_ms_p99 declared
- [x] Docs updated and example manifest added

Merge outcome:
Merged by squash on 2025-11-12 after approvals and green CI. Rollout started with a dry-run and first staged apply to models with existing performance profiles.
text:
Context and motivation:
We frequently onboard new models with performance guidance buried in PR bodies or internal wiki pages. That makes it hard for the serving runtime and release automation to pick optimal defaults (quantization, batch sizing, routing policy) and for release notes tools to produce consistent changelogs. This PR introduces a small, intentionally-declarative annotation layer on model release manifests and ties those annotations into the changelog generator and onboarding checks.

What this PR does (high-level):
1) Adds a new optional top-level 'release_annotations' block to model manifest schema. The block is expressive but constrained: keys include "recommended_quant", "latency_ms_p99", "preferred_batching" (list), "compat_notes", and "release_reason".
2) Implements a parser and validator (registry/annotations/validator.py) that enforces allowed keys, ranges (e.g., latency_ms_p99 > 0), and cross-field constraints (e.g., preferred_batching values must be one of ["single", "small", "large"]).
3) Extends the changelog assembler to consume release_annotations and produce a structured release blurb that highlights runtime hints and a concise change summary. The assembler now emits a short 'runtime_hints' section in the generated changelog JSON and prose.
4) Adds a migration utility (tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py) that scans existing manifests and emits a report + patch bundle with conservative default hints derived from existing performance profiles when available.
5) Connects annotations to the release gating pipeline: models with annotations that declare latency_ms_p99 will have additional automated smoke tests configured during CI to validate expected 95/99 latency under a representative micro-benchmark.
6) Adds docs and examples (docs/onboard/release-annotations.md) and CLI support (cli/modelctl annotate-release --file ...) for maintainers to add or update annotations.

Design notes and tradeoffs:
- Annotations are optional and backwards compatible. No manifest without annotations will be rejected. The migration tool encourages progressive adoption.
- Values are intentionally conservative; e.g., default recommended_quant is 'q4_0' where no fine-grained profiling exists. Teams with tighter SLOs can override.
- We avoid trying to express every runtime knob in the manifest to keep the contract stable. The annotation surface focuses on small, high-value hints that both serving and release automation can consume.

Commits (summary):
- 7f2d9b2: add schema extension and parser for release_annotations
- 02b4e6c: implement validator and unit tests for constraints and error messages
- 3a9d4c1: integrate annotations into changelog assembler and prose formatter
- bc12fda: migration tool to backfill annotations from existing profiles; dry-run mode
- f0e1a77: add CLI commands and docs; examples and e2e smoke tests
- e8c3b44: hook CI smoke test step to require latency check if latency_ms_p99 declared

Changed files (high level):
- registry/schema/model_manifest_v2.yaml (schema change)
- registry/annotations/validator.py (new)
- registry/annotations/parser.py (new)
- tooling/changelog/assembler.py (update to consume annotations)
- tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py (new migration)
- cli/modelctl/commands.py (annotate subcommand)
- tests/annotations/test_validator.py (unit tests)
- tests/e2e/test_release_annotation_smoke.py (CI smoke test harness)
- docs/onboard/release-annotations.md (docs)
- examples/onboard/sample-model-with-annotations.yaml (example manifest)

Review conversation (high-level):
- Marcus Patel (review): "Like the direction. Two asks: (1) ensure validator failure messages are actionable (line+example), (2) add a note about how runtime will treat unknown keys."
- Ava Chen (author): "Addressed: validator messages now include manifest path/line where available and validator ignores unknown keys with a warning (configurable). Updated tests to exercise unknown-key behavior."
- Lucia García (review): "Changelog output looks good; can we make the prose a single sentence if the runtime_hints set is small? Also, add a migration dry-run report format so SRE can audit changes before apply."
- Ava Chen: "Prose single-sentence collapse implemented; migration tool now emits dry-run JSON report and a human-readable patch summary. Added docs link in PR body."
- Daniel Kim (approve): "Verified e2e smoke test passes on my local run and CI. Approving."

CI and checks:
- Unit tests: pass (all annotations tests green)
- Lint: pass
- Changelog assembler integration test: pass
- E2E smoke (latency check): pass on CI; note: two flaky runs previously due to infra noise, resolved by pinning micro-benchmark seed and increasing sampling window.

Migration and rollout plan:
- Step 1 (dry-run): run tools/migrations/backfill_release_annotations.py --dry-run across the registry, produce a review bundle and post to Slack channel #model-onboard for sign-off.
- Step 2 (staged apply): apply patches for low-risk models (models with existing perf profiles) and validate with CI smoke tests.
- Step 3 (full apply): after one week of signals and follow-ups, apply remaining conservative defaults and notify teams to refine annotations.

Follow-ups and TODOs (tracked as small follow-up tickets):
- Add an API endpoint for the console to render runtime_hints on model pages. (ENG-4794)
- Explore auto-suggesting preferred_batching based on historical traffic patterns.
- Consider mapping release annotations to automatic routing policies for Dedicated customers in a follow-up PR.

Why this is different from prior work:
Several prior PRs touched changelog assembly or profile stamping; this change centralizes runtime-relevant hints in a compact, auditable manifest extension and ties those hints directly to both release blurb generation and CI smoke tests. It reduces ad-hoc documentation and makes serving/runtime choices more discoverable and programmatic.

QA checklist (in PR body):
- [x] Schema changes validated with unit tests
- [x] Backfill migration dry-run produces expected patches
- [x] Changelog prose includes runtime_hints when present
- [x] E2E smoke latency checks pass when latency_ms_p99 declared
- [x] Docs updated and example manifest added

Merged by squash on 2025-11-12 after approvals and green CI. Rollout started with a dry-run and first staged apply to models with existing performance profiles.

source.title: Migrate model records to metadata v1 (backfill + rollback plan)
source.author: Michael Nguyen
source.release_notes: No customer-visible behavior change intended. Adds metadata_v1 storage + backfill and keeps legacy fields readable during rollout.
source.body: Context: As part of the model metadata schema v1 rollout (see pr-18421), we need to migrate existing model/variant records so the registry can serve metadata_v1 consistently and unblock changelog generation + Console change history. Today, key fields are split across legacy columns and ad-hoc JSON; some records are incomplete and some are deployment-specific. This PR introduces a forward-only schema migration, a backfill job, and an explicit rollback procedure that preserves read-compatibility during the rollout window. Summary of changes: 1) DB schema: add metadata_v1 columns (jsonb) for model_versions and model_variants, plus schema_hash and migration bookkeeping (backfilled_at, backfill_source, backfill_warnings). Add partial indexes for common queries (model_id + version, availability filters). 2) Backfill job: new admin command `registry-admin backfill-metadata-v1` that transforms legacy fields into metadata v1 shape using the v1 schema library. Supports dry-run, batching, concurrency=1 by default, and idempotency (safe to re-run). Emits structured logs and counters per phase. 3) Safety checks: the backfill performs validation against the v1 JSON Schema, with strict vs permissive modes. Strict mode fails the run; permissive mode writes metadata_v1 plus warning annotations and a reason code. Includes a blocklist for known-bad legacy records (write no-op + emit alert) so we don’t half-migrate. 4) Read path compatibility: the registry read API continues to serve legacy fields, and adds `metadata_v1` on the response when present. No consumers are switched in this PR. A feature flag `MODEL_METADATA_V1_READ_PREFERRED` (default false) is included for staging-only verification. 5) Rollback plan: documented steps to revert reads to legacy-only, and to remove/ignore partially written metadata_v1 safely. Because the migration is additive, rollback is primarily a configuration rollback; the backfill writes can remain without impacting existing callers. Implementation details: Mapping logic is in `internal/metadata/migrate/legacy_to_v1.ts` and is intentionally conservative: (a) version: derives `version.semver` if present; otherwise sets `version.scheme=registry-seq` and preserves the legacy version string in `internal.legacy_version`. (b) tokenizer/runtime: best-effort inference from existing model_family + engine fields; if unknown, sets `compatibility.requires_human_review=true` and adds a warning. (c) availability: populates Hosted/Dedicated/Private availability based on legacy deployment flags and region allowlists; defaults to "unknown" rather than assuming global availability. (d) deprecations: carries forward existing deprecation_date fields; if only a free-form note exists, it is placed under internal.deprecation_context and not made public. Metrics/monitoring added: `model_registry.metadata_v1.backfill.records_total` (tagged by entity=model_version|variant, result=written|skipped|failed), `model_registry.metadata_v1.backfill.validation_failures_total` (tagged by reason), `model_registry.metadata_v1.backfill.duration_ms` (per batch), and `model_registry.metadata_v1.backfill.warnings_total` (tagged by warning_code). Also adds a one-time gauge report in logs at end-of-run: counts by model_family and deployment mode to help spot skew. Ops runbook (included in this PR): - Recommended rollout sequence: (1) deploy additive schema; (2) run backfill in staging with dry-run + strict mode; (3) run staging with strict write; (4) enable `MODEL_METADATA_V1_READ_PREFERRED` in staging and compare API diffs; (5) run production backfill in permissive mode with alerting; (6) follow up on warnings and re-run strict mode for clean-up. - Batch sizing guidance: start with 500 records/batch, pause between batches if DB CPU > 65% for 5m. - Alert thresholds: if failed > 0.5% in any 10m window or validation_failures spikes, page. - Success criteria: 99.9% records written or intentionally skipped with known warning codes; 0 schema validation failures in strict mode after remediation. Rollback steps (documented under `docs/ops/metadata-v1-migration.md`): 1) Disable `MODEL_METADATA_V1_READ_PREFERRED` everywhere (or confirm it remains false). 2) Stop/rollback any downstream consumers that switched to metadata_v1 reads (none in this PR). 3) If the backfill is causing DB load, stop the job; already-written metadata_v1 is safe to keep. 4) If we must revert writes, run `registry-admin backfill-metadata-v1 --revert` which clears metadata_v1 only for records written by this job (checks backfill_source and backfilled_at window), leaving any manual edits intact. 5) Post-rollback: export warning/failure report to Slack #eng-releases and attach to ENG-9822 for follow-up. Testing: Adds unit tests for legacy->v1 mapping for common model families and known edge cases (missing tokenizer, non-semver versions, quantization variants). Adds an integration test that runs the backfill against a seeded test DB and verifies idempotency and performance (bounded queries, no full table scans). Includes a regression test for the case raised in SUP-28429 (need exact change history later) by ensuring we preserve legacy identifiers in internal fields for traceability. Notes for reviewers: - The goal here is correctness + safety, not perfect metadata completeness. We prefer warnings over guessing. - Please focus on: (a) DB migration safety (locks/index builds), (b) idempotency + revert correctness, and (c) whether warning codes are actionable for follow-up. - Connor: release train implications are in the runbook; this should land before we turn on CI schema-diff checks in other repos.
commits: db(migrations): add metadata_v1 columns + indexes for model records
admin(backfill): implement legacy_to_v1 transform + idempotent writer
validation: run JSON Schema validation with strict/permissive modes
ops: add rollback/revert command and migration runbook
metrics: emit counters/timers for backfill health + warnings
tests: mapping fixtures + seeded DB integration test
ci_checks: migration safety check (no long exclusive locks): pass
source.review_conversation: Quentin Harris (review): Mapping looks reasonable, but I’m worried about silently synthesizing semver for legacy versions. Can we make sure we never fabricate a semver when the legacy field is non-semver? Also, can you include a warning code for "version_non_semver" so we can chase those down before we expose public release notes? Michael Nguyen (author): Agreed. I removed the "try to coerce" path. If legacy version doesn’t match semver, we set version.scheme=registry-seq and stash legacy string in internal.legacy_version + emit warning_code=version_non_semver. Added tests for a couple known offenders. Ethan Park (review): DB migration: are the new indexes created concurrently? We’ve been burned by blocking writes on large tables. Also, the integration test uses `CREATE INDEX` without CONCURRENTLY; can we keep prod migration safe? Michael Nguyen (author): Yes—prod migration uses `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` where allowed; for the one partial index that can’t be concurrent in our migration framework, I added a follow-up to build it out-of-band and left it out of the migration. Updated docs with the exact commands. Connor O'Brien (review): Rollback plan is good; can you add a clear "stop conditions" section and who to page if validation failures exceed threshold? Also please ensure the revert command only clears rows written by this job (don’t wipe manual edits). Michael Nguyen (author): Added stop conditions + paging instructions (Release Eng on-call, then Platform DB on-call). Revert checks backfill_source=="metadata_v1_backfill_v1" and backfilled_at within provided window; it refuses to run without both. Sahana Iyer (review): CI: the seeded DB integration test initially failed on my branch due to timing/flake. Can we reduce batch size in tests and add deterministic ordering? Michael Nguyen (author): Done—test uses batch_size=50 and orders by primary key; also added retry around transient connection reset in CI. Re-ran CI 3x. Quentin Harris (approval): Approved after warning code changes + semver handling fix. Ethan Park (approval): Approved; thanks for addressing index safety. Connor O'Brien (approval): Approved; runbook is clear enough to hand to the release train.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash into main
follow_ups: ENG-9822: run staging backfill in strict mode and publish warning report
ENG-9822: decide remediation process for version_non_semver warnings
ENG-9826: wire release-note preview CI to require metadata_v1 present for promotions (blocked on this migration landing)

source.title: introduce-model-accession-canonicalization-and-release-tape
source.author: Hannah Ortega
source.description:
Motivation: onboarding produced multiple informal identifiers for the same model (aliases, git tags, local upload ids). That ambiguity propagates into changelogs, rollback tooling, and audit exports. This PR introduces a compact canonical 'Model Accession' (MA) assigned at onboarding and a small, signed 'release tape' artifact that bundles the accession, profile snapshot, and a provenance hash for release-note automation.

What this PR does: 1) Adds an accession generator and persistence layer: a short, globally-unique accession (MA-YYYY-<base32>) recorded on model create. 2) Adds a release-tape assembler (lightweight JSONL artifact) that contains the accession, model metadata canonicalized, performance-profile snapshot, and a provenance hash signed by the registry key. 3) Adds 'profile guards' to validate incoming performance profiles against accountable thresholds (latency P95, mem footprint, token-cost estimate). Guards are pluggable and recorded on the tape. 4) Backfill/migration: a conservative backfill will assign accessions to existing models without changing their public aliases; release-tapes are generated only for new/updated onboard events during the feature-flag rollout. 5) CLI + API: exposes onboarding flag --emit-release-tape and a registry endpoint to fetch latest tape by accession. 6) Docs and decision record describing accession format, signing, and retention policy.

Compatibility and rollout: feature-flag guarded (registry.enable_accession=true). Default behavior is no-op for existing clients. Release-tape is optional; consumers can request the tape if they need authoritative changelog snippets. Backfill runs as a low-priority job, writing accessions to a new optional column so existing identifiers remain the source-of-truth for user-facing surfaces until teams opt-in.

Performance: accession generation is cheap (local deterministic stamp + random bytes), release-tape assembly occurs asynchronously during onboarding to avoid slowing hot paths; initial performance tests show onboarding CPU walltime +3% and no noticeable change to cold-start latency.

Security and provenance: the tape is signed with the registry's long-lived registry key; verification helpers added to the client SDKs. Signing signature and provenance hash are recorded in audit logs for compliance.

Migration plan and safety: 1) Deploy accession generator behind flag. 2) Run backfill in read-only mode to preview accession collisions (none expected). 3) Flip flag to start emitting tapes for new onboard events. 4) Run consumer integration tests for tooling relying on canonical ids. 5) Rollout to full cohort.

Testing: unit tests for accession uniqueness and determinism, integration tests for tape assembly+signature verification, e2e backfill dry-run. Also manual validation on a staging dataset of ~1.2k models.

Notes: this intentionally keeps the public alias surface unchanged. Accessions are intended to be a stable internal anchor for releases, rollbacks, and changelog automation; they are not meant to replace human-friendly names.

Related: ENG-8732 (canonical model ids), ENG-9104 (release automation).
commits: feat(accession): add accession generator and persistence schema (db migration + models)
feat(release-tape): implement assembler and signature helper, async worker trigger
chore(migration): add backfill dry-run job and CLI hooks
feat(profile-guards): pluggable guard interface and default thresholds
docs: add decision record and user-docs for accession and tape
test: add unit/integration/e2e coverage for accession/tape
review_comments: Marcus Li: Please add a unique index on the accession column in the migration — we need DB-level guarantees, not only app checks.
Hannah Ortega: Added migration with unique index and a small integration test for concurrent onboarding (see migrations/2025_10_07_add_accession_column.sql).
Priya Nair: Nit: rename 'releaseTape' -> 'release_tape' to align with existing package names. Also add an example tape in docs for auditing teams.
Hannah Ortega: Renamed package and added example tape JSON in docs. Also added tape verification helper to python SDK.

title: introduce-model-accession-canonicalization-and-release-tape
preview:
Motivation: onboarding produced multiple informal identifiers for the same model (aliases, git tags, local upload ids). That ambiguity propagates into changelogs, rollback tooling, and audit exports. This PR introduces a compact canonical 'Model Accession' (MA) assigned at onboarding and a small, signed 'release tape' artifact that bundles the accession, profile snapshot, and a provenance hash for release-note automation.

What this PR does: 1) Adds an accession generator and persistence layer: a short, globally-unique accession (MA-YYYY-<base32>) recorded on model create. 2) Adds a release-tape assembler (lightweight JSONL artifact) that contains the accession, model metadata canonicalized, performance-profile snapshot, and a provenance hash signed by the registry key. 3) Adds 'profile guards' to validate incoming performance profiles against accountable thresholds (latency P95, mem footprint, token-cost estimate). Guards are pluggable and recorded on the tape. 4) Backfill/migration: a conservative backfill will assign accessions to existing models without changing their public aliases; release-tapes are generated only for new/updated onboard events during the feature-flag rollout. 5) CLI + API: exposes onboarding flag --emit-release-tape and a registry endpoint to fetch latest tape by accession. 6) Docs and decision record describing accession format, signing, and retention policy.

Compatibility and rollout: feature-flag guarded (registry.enable_accession=true). Default behavior is no-op for existing clients. Release-tape is optional; consumers can request the tape if they need authoritative changelog snippets. Backfill runs as a low-priority job, writing accessions to a new optional column so existing identifiers remain the source-of-truth for user-facing surfaces until teams opt-in.

Performance: accession generation is cheap (local deterministic stamp + random bytes), release-tape assembly occurs asynchronously during onboarding to avoid slowing hot paths; initial performance tests show onboarding CPU walltime +3% and no noticeable change to cold-start latency.

Security and provenance: the tape is signed with the registry's long-lived registry key; verification helpers added to the client SDKs. Signing signature and provenance hash are recorded in audit logs for compliance.

Migration plan and safety: 1) Deploy accession generator behind flag. 2) Run backfill in read-only mode to preview accession collisions (none expected). 3) Flip flag to start emitting tapes for new onboard events. 4) Run consumer integration tests for tooling relying on canonical ids. 5) Rollout to full cohort.

Testing: unit tests for accession uniqueness and determinism, integration tests for tape assembly+signature verification, e2e backfill dry-run. Also manual validation on a staging dataset of ~1.2k models.

Notes: this intentionally keeps the public alias surface unchanged. Accessions are intended to be a stable internal anchor for releases, rollbacks, and changelog automation; they are not meant to replace human-friendly names.

Related: ENG-8732 (canonical model ids), ENG-9104 (release automation).

source.title: Introduce ephemeral onboarding probe and annotated change tester
source.author: Marisol Vega
source.description:
Motivation: onboarding new models currently records static profiles and full-sample performance snapshots which are heavy and slow. Teams often need a lightweight, low-cost sanity pass that validates metadata, short sequence performance, and quick compatibility checks without generating full canonical profiles. This PR adds two complementary pieces: (1) an ephemeral probe runner (epprobe) that executes short synthetic and sampled real workloads against a candidate model to produce a concise validation artifact, and (2) an annotated change tester that computes a delta against the previous release snapshot and drafts suggested changelog impact lines (latency, memory, tokenizer incompatibilities) for reviewers.

What this change does: epprobe is a small CLI + service hook that runs a configurable set of micro-benchmarks (short sequences, prefix cache warmups, tokenization sanity) and emits a signed, compact validation record stored in the onboarding bucket. The annotated change tester consumes existing model snapshots and epprobe records to compute a human-readable impact summary and a machine label (safe/regressive/unknown) which is surfaced as an auto-draft in the model release UI. The change includes: lightweight protobuf schemas for probe results, a new onboarding pipeline step, a UI draft generator, and a test harness with synthetic scenarios.

Goals/constraints: keep footprint small (no large profile uploads by default), ensure probe runs cannot access private customer data (uses anonymized or synthetic samples), make suggested changelog lines conservative and review-first (never auto-publish without human approval).

How to review: focus on the probe runner CLI, the compact protobuf schema, the delta heuristics in annotated_tester.py, and the minimal UI integration. The PR intentionally avoids changing historical profile storage paths and leaves full-profile generation opt-in.

Related: ENG-1472 adds the lightweight probe RFC; SUP-4121 is a customer request for faster onboarding feedback on new private models.

b7c8d9e: feat(tester): annotated change tester and delta heuristics
e8f9a0b: perf(tests): add synthetic harness and CI scenarios
f1e2d3c: ci: wire epprobe into onboarding pipeline step

review_thread: Ethan Park: Nice approach. Can epprobe run on Dedicated nodes without affecting quotas?
Marisol Vega: Epprobe uses a capacity-limited job queue and marks runs as best-effort; Dedicated pools are consulted via a low-priority tag. Added note in runner and docs.
Priya Shah: The heuristics look conservative but I'm concerned about false positives for tokenizer mismatches. Can we add a threshold override?
Marisol Vega: Added a threshold config and UI toggle (defaults preserved). Updated tests to cover edge case where tokenization differs only in BOS/EOS handling.
Liam O'Connor: CI failed once due to a flaky timing test; rebase + retry fixed it. Approving after CI green.
Priya Shah: Approved with one docs nit (clarify what 'signed' means for probe records).
Marisol Vega: Clarified in docs and added a short note in schema comment — signing uses platform key and is audit-only (not encryption).
source.qa_notes: Manual validation steps included: 1) run epprobe locally against a small model mirror, 2) confirm probe artifact uploaded to onboarding-bucket/test, 3) run annotated_tester against the probe artifact and previous snapshot and verify autodraft in UI.
source.release_notes: Add ephemeral probe runner and annotated change tester: lightweight onboarding validation and auto-draft changelog suggestions (review required). This is opt-in for full profiling and non-breaking.
follow_up_tasks: ENG-1503: Add scheduled nightly epprobe sampling for selected model families
ENG-1511: Add more tokenizer edge-case detectors to annotated tester
DOCS-99: Expand onboarding docs with walkthrough for Private deployments

source.title: Introduce cohorted intake sampler and annotated release bundler
source.author: Maya Chen
source.release_notes: Adds a cohort-driven sampling component for model intake and a release bundler that emits annotated changelog snippets, provenance hashes, and cohort coverage metrics. Improves onboarding visibility and reduces noisy release notes for large model updates.
source.description: Motivation: onboarding churn for large model uploads produced long, noisy changelogs that were hard for reviewers to triage. This PR implements a cohort-aware intake sampler that selects representative probes per model variant and a release bundler that packages compact annotated changelog snippets plus provenance and cohort-coverage metrics. Implementation: 1) ingest/cohort_sampler: deterministic cohort selection seeded by model manifest fingerprint; supports weighted cohorts, stratified sampling across datasets and input lengths, and optional sampling profiles passed from the CLI or CI. 2) bundler/release_bundler: consumes sampler output, computes a canonical provenance hash for the intake bundle, synthesizes concise changelog fragments prioritized by regression score and coverage delta, and emits a compact release bundle artifact (ndjson + metadata.json). 3) CLI: onboardctl additions to preview cohort composition and produce a dry-run changelog preview. 4) tests and docs: unit tests for sampler determinism and coverage heuristics, integration test for bundler end-to-end, and docs describing how to interpret cohort coverage and attach release snippets to model versions. Checklist: - [x] design doc approved (ENG-2046) - [x] sampler core implementation - [x] bundler compact changelog synthesis - [x] CLI preview and dry-run - [x] unit/integration tests - [x] docs and usage examples - [x] e2e smoke on staging Notes: deterministic behavior is important to allow reproducible diffs between releases; the sampler uses a stable hash over manifest fields to seed sampling. The bundler includes an optional verbosity flag to expand snippets for full audit. Links: linear/ENG-2046, support/SUP-842. CI: unit tests were flaky for the stratified sampler on first run due to an off-by-one in boundary math; fixed in commit 3. This PR avoids changing the public onboarding contract but adds metadata fields to release bundles; existing consumers will ignore unknown fields.
commits: Add cohorted sampler module and initial heuristics
Bundle release composer and provenance hash writer
CLI: onboardctl preview and dry-run output
Fix off-by-one in stratified sampler boundary calculation
Add unit and integration tests, update docs
review_comments: Ethan Park: Nice end-to-end flow, can you add a short note on how sampling interacts with existing prefix/KV caching policies?
Maya Chen: Answered - sampler returns a stable set of seed inputs; we annotate bundle metadata with recommended cache hints but do not mutate runtime cache behavior in this PR.
Priya Nair: The provenance hash should be stable across equivalent manifests; add a small fuzz test to assert invariants.
Maya Chen: Added tests asserting provenance stability for re-ordered manifest fields and for equivalent aliasing; pushed commit 4.
Riley Ford: CI showed a flaky stratified sampler test, observed an off-by-one causing one bucket to be empty at small sample sizes. Fix looks good.
Priya Nair: Approving pending a small docs tweak to explain coverage percentage semantics.
Ethan Park: Approving; left a nit about logging verbosity levels which can be a follow-up (ENG-2101).
source.merge_outcome: merged via squash merge by Maya Chen. Merge commit message: "Introduce cohorted intake sampler and release bundler (ENG-2046)". No conflicts. Backported changelog snippet generator to release/2025-09 for staging verification.
source.notes: This PR is intentionally conservative about contract changes: release bundles add new metadata keys (cohort_seed, provenance_hash, cohort_coverage) that are fully backward-compatible. The next follow-up PR will add optional sample-driven eval runners which will consume the bundles and produce per-cohort regression metrics (tracked in ENG-2101).

source.title: introduce-cohorted-affinity-change-summarizer-and-evolution-tracker
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description:
Motivation: As teams onboard more model variants and tune performance profiles, release notes become noisy and obscure the true user-facing impact. This PR adds a cohort-aware affinity summarizer and evolution tracker which groups model metadata deltas by production cohort affinity and synthesizes concise changelog snippets for release notes and the onboarding UI.

What it does (single continuous description): Adds a background worker that computes cohort affinity vectors for models (based on observed telemetry and declared affordances), records per-profile delta hashes, and generates human-readable changelog fragments that prioritize changes likely to affect high-affinity cohorts. The change includes: DB schema migration (model_profiles.affinity_vector, changelog_fragments table), API endpoints (/v1/registries/{model}/changelog-preview), a CLI subcommand (mr changelog preview --cohort <name>), a lightweight summarization template tuned for ops/PM audiences, and a small UI hook to surface the preview in the onboarding flow.

Implementation notes: - affinity vectors are computed from existing telemetry signals (usage tags, latency buckets, token distribution) and optional manual cohort tags. We avoid PII by only using anonymized aggregated counts. - The evolution tracker stores stable delta fingerprints to avoid duplicate fragments across repeated reuploads. - Changelog generation uses a deterministic template with fallback to raw metric diffs when natural language summary scores are low. - Worker runs are idempotent and rate-limited; we added a TTL and backfill window for new registries.

Checklist: - [x] DB migration and rollback script - [x] Background worker + scheduler config - [x] API route and unit tests - [x] CLI integration tests - [x] Docs: changelog preview guide + sample outputs - [x] Small UI integration for onboarding preview (feature-flagged)

Testing and CI: Initial run failed lint (long lines in summarizer). Fixed style, added unit tests for affinity normalization and snapshot dedup. Integration test simulates two cohorts (recommendation, search) and asserts summaries rank ranking-sensitive deltas first. Load-run on the benchmark lab with 10k profiles simulated; worker completed without OOM and average worker memory under 400MB.

Performance/Cost: Worker amortizes across profiles by batching affinity computations and only materializing fragments for profiles with signal-change above a threshold. In our bench runs we reduce per-profile compute by ~72% relative to naive full-diff approach.

Backward compatibility and safety: All new columns default to nullable and migrations are reversible. Feature-flag gating for the preview endpoint and UI hook. No changes to existing model-serving paths.

Release notes: "Add cohorted affinity changelog summarizer and evolution tracker: auto-generates concise reviewable release notes tailored to cohorts; includes CLI, API, background worker, and docs."

Security/Privacy: We only use aggregated telemetry and respect KMS-protected metadata. CS team reviewed the migration for sensitive column names.

Related work: builds on the signal aggregation work (ENG-1987) and the delta-fingerprint project (ENG-2633).

Rollout plan: Roll out behind the mr-changelog-preview feature flag for internal orgs, enable backfill for active registries after one week, then expose UI preview to beta customers.
source.commits:
3 commits:
- feat(cohort): add affinity vector generator and telemetry mapper (Ava Chen)
- feat(changelog): add changelog_fragments table and preview API (Ava Chen)
- test/ci: add unit/integration tests and lint fixes (Ava Chen)
diff_snippets:
--- a/src/models/model_profile.py
+++ b/src/models/model_profile.py
@@ -12,6 +12,12 @@ class ModelProfile(Base):
     hardware_affinity = Column(String, nullable=True)
+    # affinity_vector stores normalized cohort affinity weights (JSON blob of floats)
+    affinity_vector = Column(String, nullable=True)
+    # last_delta_fingerprint tracks last synthesized changelog hash
+    last_delta_fingerprint = Column(String, nullable=True)
review_comments: Diego Martinez: Please add schema docs for affinity_vector and a size cap — large blobs in profiles can cause trouble in the UI cache.
Ava Chen: Added max length validation (1024 chars) and documented in data-contracts.md; updated migration to add a check constraint.
Priya Nair: Can we be explicit about PII-safe signals used to compute affinity? Add a short note in the docs.
Ava Chen: Expanded docs/registry/changelog-preview.md with the PII notes and example inputs used in tests.
Samir Patel: Unit tests look good; one flaky integration test (preview timing) — increase timeout and mock out redis TTL in the test harness.
Ava Chen: Mocked redis TTL and increased timeout; re-ran CI.

CI: Lint failed (long lines) — author fixed.
CI: Tests passed on rerun (unit 234 passed, integration 12 passed).
source.notes_for_merger: Squash when merging. Keep migration file timestamp as-is (20250215) and ensure deploy runs the migration before enabling the feature flag in production.
source.merge_outcome: Merged to main via squash. Feature flagged; DB migration applied in canary then prod during maintenance window.
source.post_merge_tasks: 1) Monitor worker backlog metric (worker.cohort_affinity.backlog) for first 48h. 2) Run backfill for active registries via scripts/backfill_changelog_fragments.py on a low-traffic window. 3) Add a small dashboard card to registry overview for 'recent changelog fragments' (PM/Docs notified).
sample_changelog_fragments: [Search cohort] Reduced per-token latency variance by 18% after quantization tweak; recommended to pin variant v2.1 for low-latency search flows.
[Recommendations cohort] Embedding drift observed: cosine similarity baseline dropped 0.04 on 7-day rolling window — consider re-indexing or pinning older model for high-precision campaigns.

We avoid PII by only using anonymized aggregated counts. - The evolution tracker stores stable delta fingerprints to avoid duplicate fragments across repeated reuploads. - Changelog generation uses a deterministic template with fallback to raw metric diffs when natural language summary scores are low. - Worker runs are idempotent and rate-limited; we added a TTL and backfill window for new registries.

Checklist: - [x] DB migration and rollback script - [x] Background worker + scheduler config - [x] API route and unit tests - [x] CLI integration tests - [x] Docs: changelog preview guide + sample outputs - [x] Small UI integration for onboarding preview (feature-flagged)

Testing and CI: Initial run failed lint (long lines in summarizer). Fixed style, added unit tests for affinity normalization and snapshot dedup. Integration test simulates two cohorts (recommendation, search) and asserts summaries rank ranking-sensitive deltas first. Load-run on the benchmark lab with 10k profiles simulated; worker completed without OOM and average worker memory under 400MB.

Performance/Cost: Worker amortizes across profiles by batching affinity computations and only materializing fragments for profiles with signal-change above a threshold. In our bench runs we reduce per-profile compute by ~72% relative to naive full-diff approach.

Backward compatibility and safety: All new columns default to nullable and migrations are reversible. Feature-flag gating for the preview endpoint and UI hook. No changes to existing model-serving paths.

Release notes: "Add cohorted affinity changelog summarizer and evolution tracker: auto-generates concise reviewable release notes tailored to cohorts; includes CLI, API, background worker, and docs."

Security/Privacy: We only use aggregated telemetry and respect KMS-protected metadata. CS team reviewed the migration for sensitive column names.

Related work: builds on the signal aggregation work (ENG-1987) and the delta-fingerprint project (ENG-2633).

Rollout plan: Roll out behind the mr-changelog-preview feature flag for internal orgs, enable backfill for active registries after one week, then expose UI preview to beta customers.

source.title: Synthetic probe playback and release-slate generator
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description: Motivation: We need a deterministic way to replay synthetic probe results and synthesize a candidate "release slate" (compact set of suggested release notes and risk tags) for new model variants so reviewers can triage onboarding impact without running full ingestion. This PR introduces a playback engine that replays previously captured synthetic-probe traces at configurable sample densities and a release-slate generator that produces a human-readable brief with risk indicators, sample performance deltas, and suggested changelog bullets. Design: 1) probe-playback service: accepts a probe trace id and density param, replays tokens/latency/error streams against candidate model variants in a sandbox environment, and emits condensed metrics. 2) release-slate generator: takes condensed metrics + profile metadata and produces a prioritized list of release bullets, per-route latency histograms (percentiles), quality regression flags (relative to baseline), and recommended mitigation suggestions (fallback model, quantization advice, config knobs). 3) Integration hooks: a new CLI command `reg-slate --trace <id> --variants v1,v2` and a web UI preview endpoint `/slate/preview?trace_id=...` for reviewers. Backwards compatibility: playback uses the same trace schema as existing probe exporter; no changes to stored traces required. Rollout plan: deploy probe-playback as a canary behind a feature flag; enable UI preview for internal curators first; iterate on slate wording based on reviewer feedback. Performance notes: a lightweight replay at 10% density runs ~3x faster than full ingestion and produces stable delta estimates within +/-2.5% on our sampled workloads. Security and infra: sandboxed ephemeral runs with KMS-backed credentials; traces scrub PII by the existing probe-export sanitizer before playback. Tests: unit tests for trace -> condensed metrics transformation, e2e tests for CLI and preview endpoint, and a small benchmark harness that validates density vs accuracy trade-offs. Checklist: [x] unit tests added [x] e2e CLI test added [x] feature-flag gating [x] docs stub updated. Related work: builds on earlier probe sampling and changelog summarizers but moves decisioning into a deterministic replay + slate generation flow to speed reviewer cycles.
commits: Init playback service and proto definitions (m.chen)
Implement condensed-metrics aggregator and percentile summarizer (m.chen)
Add CLI `reg-slate` command and preview endpoint (m.chen)
Telemetry: emit slate-generation events and density perf metrics (s.ramos)
Tests: add unit/e2e/benchmark harnesses (e.park)
Docs: add README and usage examples for internal reviewers (l.oconnor)
review_comments: Ethan Park (2025-02-15): Nice end-to-end. Two asks: (1) make density param expressive (absolute count or percent) and validate upper bounds; (2) add a brief explaining how slate risk tags are derived so reviewers understand false-positive risk.
Maya Chen (2025-02-15): Reply: added density validation and extended docs to detail risk heuristics (see docs/internal/release-slate.md).
Sofia Ramos (2025-02-16): Telemetry looks good but please ensure events don't include raw traces. Prefer event IDs and metric summaries only.
Maya Chen (2025-02-16): Acknowledged, updated slate_events.proto to reference trace_id only and added condensed metric fields.
Liam O'Connor (2025-02-17): Found a flake in bench when running on CI GPU runners; added a retry and a more stable timer in bench harness. Approving after CI green.
Ethan Park (2025-02-18): Approval: looks solid and the density vs accuracy numbers are compelling. Recommend incremental rollout.
source.merge_outcome: Merged by squash on 2025-02-20. Commit message: 'probe(playback): add deterministic playback and release-slate generator; docs and tests'
source.release_notes: Added a deterministic synthetic-probe playback service and an automated release-slate generator to speed variant onboarding reviews. Provides density-configurable replays, condensed metrics, and suggested changelog bullets. Initially gated behind a feature flag for internal review.

source.title: Add benchmark serializer and release timeliner
source.author: Ava Chen
source.release_notes: Adds a compact benchmark serializer and a release timeliner that compiles per-model micro-release benchmarks into a chronological digest for changelogs and regression triage.
source.description:
Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduces a small, portable 'benchmark serializer' and a 'release timeliner' service that composes micro-release entries into a chronological digest for changelogs and downgrade/rollout playbooks.

What: single PR that implements: (1) bench serializer (binary+JSON compact representation with v1 header and stable field ordering); (2) timeliner CLI and library that consumes serialized benchmark artifacts and emits a microrelease timeline (JSONL) and a condensed markdown digest for release notes; (3) CI hook to assemble timeline artifacts and attach a release digest draft to the linear ticket; (4) tests and docs.

Design notes: serializer is intentionally conservative: header = {format: "rdw.bench", version: "v1", model_id, variant, timestamp, schema_hash}. Payload stores compact arrays for histo buckets and numeric envelopes for p50/p90/p95/p99 and token-throughput samples. Strings are interned; field order is fixed to ensure deterministic hashes across languages. Timeliner groups artifacts by model and semantic version, applies heuristics to label regressions (>= 10% P95 regression and certainty heuristic based on sample counts), and emits per-release summary sentences plus a minimal YAML frontmatter so docs tooling can assemble it into release notes.

Checklist: [x] bench serializer implementation and roundtrip tests; [x] timeliner CLI + library; [x] deterministic hashing and stable ordering tests; [x] CI integration for release digest assembly; [x] docs and usage examples; [x] migration plan (no DB schema change; serializer stored as blobs under existing bench_artifacts table).

Commits summary: - 8d3f9b0: initial bench serializer and schema v1; - b4c12e1: add deterministic hashing and interning map; - c1a7f43: timeliner library + CLI + grouping heuristics; - ef2d4c9: add CI release hook and draft generator; - 3a7bce8: tests, docs, and examples.

Files touched (high-level): src/serializer/bench_serializer.py; src/timeliner/compile_timeline.py; cli/commands/timeliner.py; tests/test_bench_serializer.py; tests/test_timeliner_integration.py; docs/registry/release-timeliner.md; ci/release-timeliner-hook.yml; migrations/2025_02_add_bench_blob.sql; examples/benchmarks/sample_model_v1.bench.jsonl.

Review conversation (high level): 
- Diego: "This looks promising — two asks: ensure the serializer is language-portable (we may need a protobuf shim) and make the P95 regression threshold configurable via release policy."
- Ava: "Agree on portability. Kept v1 as a JSON-friendly compact blob; added a tiny proto shim in serializer/proto_shim.py and a schema_hash field so future versions can include protobuf encoding while keeping v1 header. Added --regression-threshold flag and policy config for timeliner."

- Priya: "Can we avoid blowing up storage? How big are typical serialized artifacts?"
- Ava: "Roundtrip tests show typical artifact ~1.2–3.4 KB once interned and compressed; CI stores gzipped blobs by default. We also added a retention hint and recommend registry retention policy for 'microrelease' artifacts (30/90/365)."

- Luca: "Please add more integration tests that simulate multiple variant arrivals and ensure timeline ordering is stable across clocks and ingestion races."
- Ava: "Added deterministic ordering using model_id+semver+timestamp and a tie-breaker of stable hash; added integration test 'test_timeliner_integration.py' that fuzzes arrival order."

CI notes: initial pipeline failed lint (black/flake8) and had a flaky test; fixed formatting and increased test timeout on CI; final run: all unit/integration tests passed, docs build succeeded, release hook validated.

Migration / Compatibility: no DB schema migrations needed. The serializer blobs are stored in the existing bench_artifacts table under new column 'artifact_blob' (added via non-disruptive migration script included but not required for storage backend that uses object store). Timeliner is opt-in: registry config key 'timeliner.enabled' defaults to false until feature roll is complete. v1 header allows future encodings; tooling will ignore unknown fields gracefully.

How to use (examples): 
- Serialize a benchmark: python -m src.serializer.bench_serializer --input samples/raw_latency.json --output sample_model_v2.bench
- Compile a timeline: redwood-registry timeliner compile --input-dir artifacts/ --output timeline-2025-02-17.jsonl --md-out release-digest-2025-02-17.md --regression-threshold 0.1

Why this is different from prior work: previous PRs focused on profile ingestion and release blurb generation in prose; this PR standardizes a compact, deterministic binary-friendly artifact format intended for long-lived storage and automated timeline assembly. It bridges low-level benchmark artifacts with user-facing release draft automation and enables machine-readable regression signals that can feed the alerting playbooks in SRE.

Merge: squash and merge after approvals. Merge message: "Add compact benchmark serializer (v1) + release timeliner; CLI, CI hook, tests, docs."
commits: 8d3f9b0 initial bench serializer and schema v1
b4c12e1 add deterministic hashing and interning map
c1a7f43 timeliner library + CLI + grouping heuristics
ef2d4c9 add CI release hook and draft generator
3a7bce8 tests, docs, and examples
review_conversation: Diego Morales: please ensure language portability; add proto shim — addressed in proto_shim.py
Priya Natarajan: storage size concerns — added gzipped storage recommendation and retention hints
Luca Romano: ordering/fuzz tests for ingest races — added deterministic tie-breaker and integration test
Diego Morales: approve after proto shim and config flag for threshold — approved
Priya Natarajan: approve post docs update — approved
Luca Romano: approved
ci_details: CI first run: lint failed (formatting) — fixed
CI second run: one flaky test timing out — increased timeout and re-ran
Final CI: all unit and integration tests passed; docs build passed; release hook validated

source.notes_for_backport: No backport required for older branches; feature is opt-in. If backporting, include proto_shim and retention guidance.
