- [x] changelog assembler plugin: registry/hooks/compat_snippets.py
- [x] CLI preview: registry/cli/onboard_preview.py --staged-snippets
- [x] unit tests for signature calculation and snippet heuristics
- [x] docs: docs/onboard-lifecycle.md with examples and rollout guidance

Design notes: the lifecycle_signature is intentionally compact (hex-encoded 12 bytes + version tag) to remain stable in manifests and to avoid bloating release bundles. The staged compatibility snippet is a short paragraph and a suggested remap table (previous_alias -> recommended_variant) to help platform teams stage rollouts. The heuristics aim for conservative suggestions (only propose remaps when the signature delta + perf delta exceed thresholds).

Why this is different from profile hashes: profile hashes are content fingerprints; lifecycle_signature encodes a small set of compatibility-relevant attributes and a stability score to be used by release tooling and console UI for clearer onboarding narratives.

Backward compatibility and migration: existing profiles without lifecycle_signature are treated as 'unknown' and will get a computed signature on the next onboard. The migration is non-blocking; the assembler falls back to legacy blurb generation if signature is absent.

Operational considerations: the plugin adds a single CPU-bound computation during onboarding and a fast, cached path in changelog assembly. We added a short TTL cache keyed by model_id+head_sha to avoid recomputation for large batch uploads.

Risks and mitigations: initial heuristics may be conservative and under-suggest remaps; we added a monitoring metric (registry.lifecycle.signer.suggestions.count) and a small sampling telemetry to evaluate precision.

How to test locally: run `pytest tests/test_lifecycle_signatures.py::test_signature_determinism` and `python -m registry.cli.onboard_preview --file sample_profile.json --staged-snippets` to see generated snippet output.

Related: ENG-7421 (project to tighten onboarding churn), PM-318 (product request for clearer release notes).
text:
Background: onboarding churn and subtle compatibility regressions have made changelogs noisy and hard to consume. We need a small, machine-readable lifecycle signal attached to model profiles so release automation can produce concise, actionable changelog snippets when a model moves between lifecycle phases (onboard -> staging -> prod). This PR introduces 'lifecycle_signature' metadata on model profiles and a staged compatibility-snippet generator that outputs human-friendly changelog fragments and suggested fallback mappings for deployments.

What this PR does: introduces a new lightweight lifecycle_signature schema (versioned), adds an onboarding hook to compute the signature, adds a changelog assembler plugin that emits staged compatibility snippets, and wires a CLI flag to preview generated snippets. The generator uses a deterministic fingerprint of (architecture, tokenizer_hash, primary_quant, perf_token_summary) and a short delta heuristic to summarize compatibility impact and performance deltas.

Checklist:
- [x] lifecycle_signature schema and migration support
- [x] onboarding hook: registry/hooks/lifecycle_signer.py
- [x] changelog assembler plugin: registry/hooks/compat_snippets.py
- [x] CLI preview: registry/cli/onboard_preview.py --staged-snippets
- [x] unit tests for signature calculation and snippet heuristics
- [x] docs: docs/onboard-lifecycle.md with examples and rollout guidance

source.title: Introduce onboarding contract mapper and traffic-weighted release-notes generator
source.author: Priya Nair
source.description:
Context / motivation: model onboarding churn has made release notes noisy: small metadata-only updates (typo fixes, curator comments) and large perf-affecting changes appear at the same verbosity level. Teams want concise, signal-rich changelogs that call out performance and runtime compatibility changes while suppressing low-impact edits. This PR adds two complementary pieces: (1) an onboarding contract mapper that normalizes and stamps the metadata that matters for serving/runtime compatibility, and (2) a traffic-weighted release-notes generator that scores deltas based on historical usage and staged rollout traffic to prioritize changelog sections.

What this PR does (high level):
- Introduce a lightweight onboarding_contract_mapper that: canonicalizes fields (model.aliases, quantization_profiles, runtime_hints), emits a stability fingerprint, and records a "compatibility_contract" object (stringified map) that downstream systems use for gating. The mapper runs in the intake path and as a pre-commit hook for model manifests.
- Add a traffic_weighted_changelog generator service that: consumes delta events and model traffic signals (ingest from telemetry snapshots), computes an impact_score that weights changes by percent-traffic and latency/cost delta, and outputs a compact release note with prioritized bullets (Performance, Runtime Compatibility, Behavioral, Metadata). The generator also groups low-impact deltas into an "Other metadata edits" bucket to reduce noise.
- Provide migration tooling: a one-off migrator that rehydrates existing model profiles with compatibility_contract keys and generates one sample traffic-weighted release digest for the last 30 days (stored as samples/release-digest-*.ndjson).
- Add unit + integration tests and CI job to validate contract_mapper idempotence and changelog ranking monotonicity.

Design notes and tradeoffs:
- We intentionally keep the compatibility_contract as a stable, compact string map to avoid exploding DB schema changes; the contract is designed to be backward-compatible for a two-release window.
- Impact scoring is heuristic (traffic percent * normalized perf delta + deprecation_penalty + security_flag), not ML-driven; we opted for deterministic, explainable rules in this iteration to keep auditability simple.
- The changelog generator does not automatically publish to user-facing release pages; it emits drafts for the release curator UI to review first. Future work could hook canary rollout metrics for live weighting.

Checklist:
- [x] Mapper implementation with canonicalization rules
- [x] Compatibility fingerprint + stamping
- [x] Changelog generator with traffic weighting and bucketing rules
- [x] Migrator and sample output artifacts
- [x] Unit tests for mapper and generator scoring
- [x] Integration tests with sample telemetry snapshot
- [x] CI job added and passing

Commits summary: single-squash merge with these commit headlines:
- feat(onboard): add onboarding_contract_mapper and canonicalizer
- feat(changelog): implement traffic_weighted_changelog service and API
- chore(migration): add rehydrate migrator and sample output generator
- test: add unit/integration tests for mapper + changelog scoring
- ci: add changelog-scoring CI job and test fixtures

Changed areas / notable files (high level):
- src/ingest/contract_mapper.py -- core canonicalization logic and fingerprinting rules
- src/ingest/rules.yaml -- canonical field mappings and normalization rules (documented)
- src/changelog/weighting.py -- impact scoring and bucketing algorithm
- src/changelog/service.py -- API to produce draft release digests
- tools/migrators/rehydrate_compat_contract.py -- one-off migrator scaffolding
- tests/test_contract_mapper.py, tests/test_changelog_scoring.py -- test coverage for idempotence and monotonicity
- ci/workflows/changelog-scoring.yml -- new CI workflow validating scoring determinism

Review conversation (high level):
- Arielle Chen (review): "Nice separation between contract and draft generator. Two asks: (1) make fingerprint stable across minor metadata edits, (2) add examples in rules.yaml for quant profiles."
- Author reply (Priya): "Addressed: fingerprint uses a whitelist of fields and salt; added quant examples + unit tests that exercise the stability property."
- Mateo Alvarez (review): "Can we avoid coupling telemetry ingestion directly in the changelog service? Suggest adding a small adapter interface so we can swap sources."
- Author reply: "Added TelemetryAdapter interface and a simple in-memory adapter for the migrator; production wiring kept in ingestion config. Also added an integration test using the adapter."
- Jonah Beck (approval): "Approach looks good; tests pass and migration artifacts useful for manual validation. Approving."

CI summary: all checks passed on merge. Notable checks:
- unit-tests (python): pass
- integration-tests (slow): pass (ran against test telemetry snapshot)
- lint: pass
- changelog-scoring-ci: pass (validates determinism across repeated runs)

Migration notes for operators: run tools/migrators/rehydrate_compat_contract.py --window 30d --out samples/ to generate sample release digests. The migrator writes ndjson artifacts and a dry-run log (migrations/log-48290-dryrun.txt). The migrator is idempotent; it will skip entries that already have a compatibility_contract stamp.

Security / privacy notes: The compatibility_contract intentionally omits consumer telemetry identifiers; the traffic weighting uses aggregated percent traffic metrics only (no PII). The migrator artifacts are stored in internal-only buckets by default.

Release notes (internal):
- Added onboarding contract mapper to canonicalize model metadata and emit a stable compatibility fingerprint.
- Added traffic-weighted release-notes generator to prioritize changelog items by inferred impact.
- Migration tool and sample outputs included for validation.

Merge outcome: squash merge, PR merged on 2024-11-12 after approvals and CI green.

feat(changelog): implement traffic_weighted_changelog service and API
chore(migration): add rehydrate migrator and sample output generator
test: add unit/integration tests for mapper + changelog scoring
ci: add changelog-scoring CI job and test fixtures
review_comments: Arielle Chen: Make fingerprint stable across minor metadata edits; add quant profile examples in rules.yaml
Priya Nair (author): Implemented whitelist + salt approach for fingerprint; added quant examples and tests
Mateo Alvarez: Add TelemetryAdapter interface to decouple ingestion from generator
Priya Nair (author): Added TelemetryAdapter and in-memory adapter for migrator; updated integration test
Jonah Beck: Approving after tests and migration artifacts validated

source.notes_for_reviewers: Focus review on: (1) contract canonicalization whitelist, (2) scoring weight constants and monotonicity tests, (3) migrator idempotence behavior. I left detailed rubric in src/changelog/README.md.
source.audits: compatibility_contract fingerprints are logged to migrations/log-48290-dryrun.txt; scoring determinism validated by changelog-scoring-ci

source.title: Introduce embargoed provenance ledger and microdigest generator
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description: Context: customers and internal curation teams have asked for a way to attach time-bound/embargoed metadata to model intake that is cryptographically auditable and gated for release notes. This PR adds a small provenance ledger that signs embargoed metadata, exposes an ACL-aware retrieval path, and generates a compact "microdigest" used by the Console and release automation to show concise delta-first release snippets. Motivation: reduce manual release-note drafting, centralize embargo handling for sensitive commercial or regulatory annotations, and produce deterministic microdigests for downstream QA and changelog automation. Changes: 1) API: intake endpoint accepts optional 'embargo_until' and 'embargo_reason' metadata; embargoed fields are stored encrypted and recorded in the provenance ledger with signer metadata. 2) Ledger: lightweight append-only ledger table with signer_id, signed_hash, ttl and visibility flags; signer service signs using an internal key (KMS-backed) and publishes short receipts. 3) Microdigest generator: new service that computes a compact human-friendly summary from model profile deltas (perf metrics, metadata fields, and affinity hints) and includes risk flags when embargoed fields are present. 4) UI: Console exposes a microdigest preview and marks embargoed items with an eye icon and 'until' date. 5) Migration: DB migration to add ledger table + retention TTL. Checklist: - [x] API schema and intake validation - [x] DB migration (ledger table + indices) - [x] signer service + KMS access scoped - [x] microdigest generator service + deterministic template - [x] Console preview + tests - [x] e2e: onboarding -> draft microdigest -> preview - [x] documentation: registry/embargoed-metadata.md and release-microdigest.md See Linear ENG-4821 for product context and SUP-1234 for a support customer ask. No external schema changes for downstream consumers; microdigest is opt-in during release assembly.
commits: c9b8a3d: add ledger table migration and index (migrations/20241105_add_provenance_ledger.sql)
d1f2b4e: intake API: accept embargoed metadata and validate embargo_until format (api/intake/handler.go)
e7a6c09: signer service: KMS integration and signed receipt format (services/signer/*)
b4f5e11: microdigest: delta extractor and templating engine (services/microdigest/engine.py)
a2c3d55: console: preview panel and embargoed item UI affordance (web/console/src/components/MicrodigestPreview.tsx)
f8d9e77: add unit + integration tests for ledger, signer, and microdigest (tests/ledger_test.go, tests/microdigest_test.py)
3a4b7f9: docs: add registry/embargoed-metadata.md and release-microdigest.md
review_conversation: Diego Ramos (reviewer) 2024-11-06: Please justify the retention TTL; we should default to 90 days unless product asks otherwise.
Ava Chen (author) 2024-11-06: TTL set to 90 days by default; field added to migration and configuration at registry.provenance.ttl_days. Updated migration accordingly.
Priya Nair (reviewer) 2024-11-08: Cryptographic receipt looks good, but can we include signer identity in the UI hover?
Ava Chen 2024-11-08: Added signer_id to the receipt and Console hover shows signer name with a link to the verification page.
Samir Patel (security) 2024-11-09: KMS-scoped key usage approved. Minor nit: rotate signer key every 30 days — opened follow-up ENG-4890.
Priya Nair 2024-11-10: Integration tests flaky on CI (microdigest templating); I ran a local reproducible fix and pushed a small change to templating (commit e7a6c09).
CI system 2024-11-11: integration tests rerun and passing.
ci_checks: integration: pass (2 runs, one flaky rerun fixed)
security-scan: pass (no elevated secrets in repo)
changes_summary: Add provenance ledger for embargoed metadata with KMS-backed signer receipts
Intake API extended with embargo_until and encrypted fields
Microdigest generator: deterministic delta-first summaries and risk flags
Console preview with embargo indicators and signer hover
DB migration and retention policy configurable via registry.provenance.ttl_days
End-to-end tests and docs to support operator rollout
source.merge_outcome: Merged to main (squash) after approvals from Diego, Priya, and Samir. No rollback required during validation window.
source.release_notes: Feature: Add embargoed provenance ledger and release microdigest generator. Operators can now attach time-limited, auditable metadata to model intake; Console and release automation will show compact microdigests that summarize performance and metadata deltas. Config: registry.provenance.ttl_days (default 90). Security: signer key rotation tracked under ENG-4890.
source.notes: Follow-up work: ENG-4890 (key rotation), ENG-4912 (allow user-level read audit logs), and PM-213 (UI copy for embargo reasons). Deployed to canary cluster 2024-11-12 and promoted to prod 2024-11-12.
source.security_considerations: Embargoed fields are encrypted at rest and only decrypted by registry service when ACL permits. Signer key usage audited via KMS logs. No secrets stored in repo. Access to signed receipts honors role-based visibility. See docs/registry/embargoed-metadata.md for operator guidance.

This PR adds a small provenance ledger that signs embargoed metadata, exposes an ACL-aware retrieval path, and generates a compact "microdigest" used by the Console and release automation to show concise delta-first release snippets. Changes: 1) API: intake endpoint accepts optional 'embargo_until' and 'embargo_reason' metadata; embargoed fields are stored encrypted and recorded in the provenance ledger with signer metadata. 2) Ledger: lightweight append-only ledger table with signer_id, signed_hash, ttl and visibility flags; signer service signs using an internal key (KMS-backed) and publishes short receipts. 3) Microdigest generator: new service that computes a compact human-friendly summary from model profile deltas (perf metrics, metadata fields, and affinity hints) and includes risk flags when embargoed fields are present. 4) UI: Console exposes a microdigest preview and marks embargoed items with an eye icon and 'until' date. 5) Migration: DB migration to add ledger table + retention TTL. Checklist: - [x] API schema and intake validation - [x] DB migration (ledger table + indices) - [x] signer service + KMS access scoped - [x] microdigest generator service + deterministic template - [x] Console preview + tests - [x] e2e: onboarding -> draft microdigest -> preview - [x] documentation: registry/embargoed-metadata.md and release-microdigest.md See Linear ENG-4821 for product context and SUP-1234 for a support customer ask. No external schema changes for downstream consumers; microdigest is opt-in during release assembly.

source.title: Introduce variant affordance annotator and delta-aware changelog composer
source.author: Priya Natarajan
release_notes: Add: Variant Affordance Annotator that tags model variants with capability/constraint hints used by release routing and compatibility checks.
Add: Delta-aware Changelog Composer that generates concise release notes by scoring profile diffs and sampling representative examples.
Tooling: CLI preview for changelog drafts and a lightweight policy to auto-suggest rollback candidates when regressions are detected.
Non-breaking: Backwards-compatible metadata fields and migration script included; no runtime contract changes for consumers.
source.description:
Context and motivation: We onboarded multiple model variants for the Q4 rollout and noticed reviewers and customers struggled to interpret subtle capability regressions across variants. Manual release notes were noisy and missed guidance for routing and mitigation. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: (1) a Variant Affordance Annotator that infers and stamps compact capability/constraint hints into variant metadata during intake, and (2) a Delta-aware Changelog Composer that synthesizes concise release notes by scoring profile deltas and surface-sampling representative examples.

What this delivers: - Affordance annotator: a modular analyzer that runs as part of the intake pipeline. It emits standardized tags such as "safety:high-sensitivity", "affordance:table-reading", "latency:snappy", "kvcache-friendly". Tags are stored on variant metadata and are consumed by routing heuristics and the release composer. - Delta-aware composer: a rule-and-score based engine that compares new vs baseline profiles (latency/throughput/semantic metrics) and outputs a compact changelog with: summary bullets, graded impact (minor/major/regressive), representative prompt-response pairs (sampled), and suggested mitigation notes (rollback candidate, alternative variant). - CLI preview: a new 'registry changelog preview' command that produces draft notes for reviewers; safe-by-default (no auto-merge) and opt-in automerge via policy.

Key implementation points: - Annotator uses an ensemble of heuristics: static signatures (model arch × tokenization), sampled perf probes, and lightweight semantic probes (subset of eval harness) to infer capability hints. - Affordance schema extends variant metadata with a small, namespaced map: "affordances.v1" (fields are additive and backward compatible). Migration: existing variants without affordances are treated as unknown and do not break routing. - Composer implements a delta scoring pipeline with tunable weights (latency, accuracy deltas, hallucination signal). It de-duplicates noise via clustering and ranks candidate bullets by estimated user impact. - Storage: changelog drafts are stored as compact artifacts with provenance links to commits, Linear IDs, and sampling seeds. - Security/compliance: no user prompt data is stored; representative examples are sanitized and sampled from synthetic probes or public datasets where applicable.

Commits (high level): - 1a2b3c: initial annotator scaffold, schema and unit tests - 2d3e4f: perf probe harness integration and sampling strategy - 3f4g5h: delta scoring engine and composer templates - 4h5i6j: CLI preview and documentation - 5i6j7k: migration script and end-to-end tests - 6j7k8l: polish, CI flakiness fixes, and sample sanitization

Files/areas changed (high level): - src/annotator/affordance/ : new annotator library, heuristics and unit tests - schemas/variant_affordances_v1.yaml : new compact schema extension - tools/cli/changelog_preview.py : CLI to preview composer output - composer/delta_engine.py : delta scoring and summarizer - ingest/pipeline_hooks.py : hook to run annotator on intake - migration/migrate_affordances.py : one-time additive migration - docs/onboarding/affordances.md : onboarding guide and examples - tests/e2e/changelog_flow.test : end-to-end flow using mock probes

Illustrative snippet (composer template):
{
  "summary": "Minor regression in table-answering latency (p95 +120ms), accuracy stable",
  "impact": "minor",
  "examples": ["(sanitized example prompt 1)", "(sanitized example prompt 2)"],
  "suggested_action": "Consider routing 'table-heavy' traffic to variant v2.1 until kvcache backfills match baseline"
}

Review conversation (condensed): - Maya Chen (reviewer): "Nice separation between annotator and composer. Two asks: 1) tighten sampling seed determinism for reproducibility, 2) add a short-circuit to skip semantic probes when running in air-gapped installs." - Author reply (Priya): "Addressed both. Sampling seed is now deterministic via registry seed service; semantic probe is gated behind a 'probe-mode' flag and disabled by default in private installs." - Diego Alvarez: "Please add a note about cost/latency trade-offs of running annotator in the hot path." - Author: "Moved heavy perf probes to async background workers; hot path keeps cheap static signatures only. Documented tradeoffs in affordances.md." - Samir Patel: "Approve after tests flake is resolved." - CI runs fixed and tests green. Final approval given.

CI summary: - unit: pass - e2e (mock probes): pass - lint: pass - integration (registry staging): pass after a rerun due to transient infra failure - total: 6/6 checks green

Backward compatibility and migration: - Schema extension is additive (affordances.v1). Existing consumers will ignore the new field if unrecognized. - Migration script annotates historical variants in batches; it's rate-limited and idempotent. - No public API signature changes.

Rollout plan: 1) Deploy annotator to staging with background-only mode (no auto-stamp). 2) Enable annotator for new onboardings and run migration on low-priority buckets. 3) Turn on composer preview for reviewers. 4) After one-week vetting, enable composer to emit changelog drafts automatically. 5) Monitor quality metrics (changelog acceptance rate, manual edits, false-positive regression tags) and rollback policy for 24/7 on-call.

Risk and mitigation: - Over-tagging: mitigated by conservative defaults and human-in-the-loop preview. - Probe cost: heavy probes run async and rate-limited; CLI preview uses cached artifacts. - Privacy: sanitized examples only; customers can opt-out of sampling.

Merge outcome: Squash-merged into main on 2024-11-12 after approvals and green CI. Linear references: ENG-2147, PM-511.

Notes for reviewers/maintainers: - If you need to tune composer weightings, see composer/config/weights.yaml. - For private installs, set PROBE_MODE=disabled to avoid network-based semantic probes. - The migration script can be run with --dry-run to inspect candidate variants before applying.

source.title: Add cohort-aware changelog enricher and perf baseline seeder
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.description: Introduce a cohort-aware changelog enrichment pipeline and a perf baseline seeder to improve release notes quality and initial performance expectations for newly onboarded model variants. The change adds an enrichment worker that consumes sampled telemetry and onboarding probe results, computes sample-weighted deltas per cohort, and emits human-readable summary assertions and suggested baseline numbers for the release composer. A lightweight SQLite indexer records cohort sample weights and fast lookup keys to avoid recomputing on every run. The PR also adds a small CLI entrypoint (rmr changelog enrich) and a seed script that writes initial baseline entries into the model registry so downstream automation and dashboards have an initial reference. Motivation: reduce manual work for release authors, provide reproducible baseline numbers for canary rollouts, and improve changelog context for customers. No breaking API changes to existing onboard manifests; the enrichment is opt-in via registry config and feature flag.
commits: a1f9c3d: services/enricher: add cohort weighting indexer and enrichment worker
b2d7e4f: tools/rmr_cli: add changelog enrich command and flags
c3e5a6b: scripts: seed_baselines.py to pre-populate registry baselines from sampled telemetry
d4f1b2a: docs/onboard: add changelog_enricher.md and configuration guidance
e5f3c9d: tests/enricher: add unit and integration tests; add CI retry for sampling flake
files_snippets: def compute_cohort_delta(samples, baseline): # returns weighted mean and stderr
INSERT INTO cohort_weights (model_id, variant, cohort_hash, weight) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?) -- used by indexer
ci_checks: integration-tests (passed after 1 retry due to sampling flake)
review_comments: Marta Kovac: Overall LGTM. Two requests: 1) ensure the indexer uses a streaming cursor to avoid high memory use for large sample dumps, 2) add a short note about privacy implications when sampling user prompts.
Priya Natarajan: Addressed both. Indexer now streams rows and an opt-out sampling flag was added. Updated docs to call out collection window and anonymization in changelog_enricher.md.
Diego Alvarez: Can the seed script be idempotent? If we run seed_baselines twice we should not duplicate entries.
Priya Natarajan: Seed script now uses upsert semantics keyed by model_id+variant+cohort_hash. Added test that runs seed twice to ensure idempotency.
Samir Patel: Integration test shows a 2s median extra latency when enrichment runs inline during onboarding; recommend running as background job for heavy models.
Priya Natarajan: Switched default to background worker with a flag to run inline for quick local dev. Noted tradeoff in docs.
Marta Kovac: Approved after changes.
Diego Alvarez: Approved.
source.notes_on_testing: Added unit tests for indexer boundary conditions and a small end-to-end CI job that runs seed_baselines against a dev registry snapshot. Integration test that hit sampling flakiness now retries up to 2 times and records flake metadata for triage.
source.release_notes: Adds cohort-aware changelog enrichment and initial perf baseline seeding. The enrichment computes sample-weighted deltas to produce concise release notes and seeds initial baseline numbers for variants to aid canary rollouts and dashboards. Opt-in via registry config and feature flag. No breaking changes.
related_tickets: ENG-4490: seed initial perf baselines from sampled telemetry
source.manual_steps: To enable enrichment for a registry: 1) set registry.config.enricher.enabled=true, 2) run scripts/seed_baselines.py --registry <url> --since 30d, 3) ensure background worker service is deployed (deploy/enricher.yml).
source.impact_summary: Improves release note clarity and provides reproducible perf baselines for newly onboarded variants. Expected to reduce time spent by release authors and reduce canary regressions. Small runtime cost if run inline; default is background worker to avoid onboarding latency.
source.audit_trail: Merged by Priya Natarajan on 2024-11-10 via squash merge. CI green. Linear links attached for feature work and follow-ups.
source.changelog_preview:
- Cohort-aware changelog enrichment: auto-generated human-readable deltas with sample-weighted numbers.
- Perf baseline seeder: populates initial baseline entries for new variants to help rollouts.

source.title: Compact probe serializer and autotag rationalizer for churn-resistant changelogs
source.author: Priya Menon
source.description:
Context and motivation: The model onboarding pipeline currently emits verbose probe payloads and naive changelog diffs which cause noisy release notes and expensive downstream storage/transfer. This PR introduces a compact probe serializer (binary + content-addressed prefixes) and an autotag rationalizer that deterministically picks minimal, stable tags for model profiles. The goal is to reduce changelog noise, make release diffs smaller, and improve the stability of model identifiers across quick re-uploads.

What this changes (high level): 1) CompactProbeSerializer: a new module that implements a size-aware serializer for probe samples. It uses a small binary encoding (protobuf-lite) combined with reservoir sampling for long prompts and optional bloom-filtered feature sketches for heavy fields so repeated telemetry is deduped early. 2) Fingerprint suffixes: content-addressed 8-byte suffixes are appended to the onboard manifest name to avoid collisions while remaining human-friendly. 3) AutotagRationalizer: a deterministic tagging engine that reduces tag churn by preferring semantic tags (capabilities, size, quantization) and only adds variant tags when the delta crosses significance thresholds. 4) Changelog Compactor: a pipeline step that collapses repetitive probe-level diffs into summarized impact briefs and exposes a compact delta used by the release composer.

Implementation notes: - Serializer is opt-in via ONBOARD_COMPACT_SERIALIZER=true. Backwards-compatible: fallback to legacy JSON payloads if disabled. - Reservoir sampling window defaults to 128 tokens per sample and is configurable per-model. - Bloom sketch uses 4 hashes / 1024 bits by default, configurable. - Tag rationalizer rules are expressed as prioritized predicates; new predicates can be added via config without data migration. - Changelog compaction runs as a streaming reducer that emits a concise summary blob (gzip + CBOR) and small provenance ledger (signed SHA256 deltas).

Checklist: - [x] Compact serializer implementation + tests - [x] Autotag rationalizer + unit rules tests - [x] Changelog compactor + integration harness - [x] Migration safety (feature flag + backward compatibility) - [x] Perf benchmark added to perf-lab (summary included below)

Benchmarks summary (perf-lab): average payload size reduced ~78% for representative onboarding samples, network transfer time reduced ~60% in our staging region, and release-note digest sizes reduced by ~5x on median workloads. CPU overhead for the serializer was ~3% on the worker process; acceptable under dedicated capacity.

Compatibility and rollout: This change is gated behind a feature flag and rollout is staged per-environment: dev -> canary -> staging -> prod. No immediate migration required for existing model profiles. Once enabled, changelog previews in the console will show the compact summary and an opt-in "expanded diff" for auditors.

Related work: ENG-4981 (changelog compaction), ENG-5023 (probe sampling), see also docs/arch/onboard-compact.md (new doc added in PR).
commits: a1b2c3d: introduce CompactProbeSerializer module and wire up feature flag
f4e5d6c: add AutotagRationalizer engine and basic rule set
9f8e7d6: implement changelog compactor streaming reducer
2b3c4d5: tests + perf harness + docs for compact onboarding
7e6f5a4: fix flake in integration test and add CI retry for flaky e2e
review_comments: Ethan Park: Can we guarantee byte-stability for fingerprint suffixes across different architectures? Suggest normalizing canonical fields before hashing.
Priya Menon (reply): We normalize date and host fields before hashing; added a note in compact_serializer.go and a unit test (commit a1b2c3d).
Sofia Reynolds: The Autotag rules look good; request a small additional rule to prefer 'quantized' only if quantization impacts mean token latency > 8ms.
Priya Menon (reply): Added the threshold rule (autotag_rules.yml) and a short perf test to assert behavior under the quantization threshold.
Luca Moretti: CI flaked on e2e once, rerun passed. Approved after small nits addressed.
SRE-bot: perf-regression check shows +3% CPU in worker under max throughput; within guardrail.
release_notes: Introduce CompactProbeSerializer to reduce onboarding payload sizes and bandwidth costs.
Add AutotagRationalizer to stabilize model profile tags and reduce changelog churn.
Changelog compactor summarizes repetitive probe diffs into concise release briefs.
Feature-flagged rollout; no breaking changes expected.

Checklist: - [x] Compact serializer implementation + tests - [x] Autotag rationalizer + unit rules tests - [x] Changelog compactor + integration harness - [x] Migration safety (feature flag + backward compatibility) - [x] Perf benchmark added to perf-lab (summary included below)

Benchmarks summary (perf-lab): average payload size reduced ~78% for representative onboarding samples, network transfer time reduced ~60% in our staging region, and release-note digest sizes reduced by ~5x on median workloads. CPU overhead for the serializer was ~3% on the worker process; acceptable under dedicated capacity.

Compatibility and rollout: This change is gated behind a feature flag and rollout is staged per-environment: dev -> canary -> staging -> prod. No immediate migration required for existing model profiles. Once enabled, changelog previews in the console will show the compact summary and an opt-in "expanded diff" for auditors.

Related work: ENG-4981 (changelog compaction), ENG-5023 (probe sampling), see also docs/arch/onboard-compact.md (new doc added in PR).
text:
Context and motivation: The model onboarding pipeline currently emits verbose probe payloads and naive changelog diffs which cause noisy release notes and expensive downstream storage/transfer. This PR introduces a compact probe serializer (binary + content-addressed prefixes) and an autotag rationalizer that deterministically picks minimal, stable tags for model profiles. The goal is to reduce changelog noise, make release diffs smaller, and improve the stability of model identifiers across quick re-uploads.

What this changes (high level): 1) CompactProbeSerializer: a new module that implements a size-aware serializer for probe samples. It uses a small binary encoding (protobuf-lite) combined with reservoir sampling for long prompts and optional bloom-filtered feature sketches for heavy fields so repeated telemetry is deduped early. 2) Fingerprint suffixes: content-addressed 8-byte suffixes are appended to the onboard manifest name to avoid collisions while remaining human-friendly. 3) AutotagRationalizer: a deterministic tagging engine that reduces tag churn by preferring semantic tags (capabilities, size, quantization) and only adds variant tags when the delta crosses significance thresholds. 4) Changelog Compactor: a pipeline step that collapses repetitive probe-level diffs into summarized impact briefs and exposes a compact delta used by the release composer.

Implementation notes: - Serializer is opt-in via ONBOARD_COMPACT_SERIALIZER=true. Backwards-compatible: fallback to legacy JSON payloads if disabled. - Reservoir sampling window defaults to 128 tokens per sample and is configurable per-model. - Bloom sketch uses 4 hashes / 1024 bits by default, configurable. - Tag rationalizer rules are expressed as prioritized predicates; new predicates can be added via config without data migration. - Changelog compaction runs as a streaming reducer that emits a concise summary blob (gzip + CBOR) and small provenance ledger (signed SHA256 deltas).

Checklist: - [x] Compact serializer implementation + tests - [x] Autotag rationalizer + unit rules tests - [x] Changelog compactor + integration harness - [x] Migration safety (feature flag + backward compatibility) - [x] Perf benchmark added to perf-lab (summary included below)

Benchmarks summary (perf-lab): average payload size reduced ~78% for representative onboarding samples, network transfer time reduced ~60% in our staging region, and release-note digest sizes reduced by ~5x on median workloads. CPU overhead for the serializer was ~3% on the worker process; acceptable under dedicated capacity.

Compatibility and rollout: This change is gated behind a feature flag and rollout is staged per-environment: dev -> canary -> staging -> prod. No immediate migration required for existing model profiles. Once enabled, changelog previews in the console will show the compact summary and an opt-in "expanded diff" for auditors.

Related work: ENG-4981 (changelog compaction), ENG-5023 (probe sampling), see also docs/arch/onboard-compact.md (new doc added in PR).

source.title: Consolidate onboard commit-capsule and auto-synthesize release briefs
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.description:
Motivation: onboarding currently scatters commit-level metadata, perf deltas, and release note fragments across artifacts and the model profile DB. That makes lightweight release summaries hard to generate and forces reviewers to cross-reference multiple sources. This change introduces a compact 'commit-capsule' blob that travels with a model profile (stored as signed metadata alongside the profile artifact) and a small pipeline to auto-synthesize concise release briefs from the capsule + perf deltas.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a commit-capsule format (minimal JSON) that contains: commit SHA, onboarding CLI user, dataset snapshot tag, top-level performance tokens (latency/throughput/embedding-l2), a short human author blurb, and a list of significant calibration flags (quantization/seq-len/kv-cache-behavior). 2) Extends the onboard CLI to emit the capsule and attach it to the profile upload payload. 3) Introduces a release-brief synthesizer service (local transformer-free template engine) that aggregates capsule metadata and latest profile diffs to produce a one-paragraph release brief plus a 3-line perf summary. 4) Migration: a migration job scans recent profiles (90 days) and attempts best-effort capsule backfill from commit messages, upload metadata, and CI artifacts. 5) Console: adds a compact 'release brief' field in the profile summary view and a download link for the capsule.

Checklist: - [x] Define capsule schema and stability guarantee. - [x] Implement CLI emit and upload hooks. - [x] Implement synthesizer service and unit tests. - [x] Add console rendering and download endpoint. - [x] Add migration job and monitoring. - [x] Add Linear tickets and docs.

Backward compatibility: capsule is additive; profiles without capsules remain readable. Synthesizer falls back to a heuristic brief if capsule missing. Signed capsules use existing model-registry signing keys; no KMS changes required.

Rollout plan: merge to main; enable synthesizer feature-flag by default in staging for 48 hours, monitor release_brief_missing_ratio and brief_length regressions. Run migration job incrementally (10% windows) to avoid API spikes.

Related: ENG-1423 (onboard metadata reliability), PM-89 (concise release notes for partner-facing changelogs).

Notes: This PR intentionally keeps the synthesizer lightweight (no heavy NLP models) to ensure predictable output and cost. Future follow-ups can add an optional LLM-based richer summarizer behind a feature flag.
source.commits:
3 commits:
- 7a2c9d9: schema: add commit-capsule v1 and validation tests
- d4be11a: cli: emit capsule and attach to profile-upload payload; add unit tests
- a8f3c72: service: release-brief synthesizer + console hook + migration job
source.changed_files: high-level: src/schema/commit_capsule_v1.go, cli/onboard/upload.go, service/synthesizer/brief_synth.go, migrations/backfill_capsule_job.go, web/console/profile_summary.tsx, tests/{schema,cli,synth}.go
source.review_comments: Liam: asked for stronger validation on 'perf_tokens' (now validated as numeric array with stable ordering). Marisol: requested user-facing example for console; added UI example and e2e test. Arjun: requested migration safety guard; added 10%-window throttling and health-check metric 'migration_backfill_rate'. Author replies clarified the fallback heuristics for missing fields and agreed to a follow-up to add schema docs. Two rounds of small fixes and another quick CI run before approval.
source.ci_details:
CI: build (pass), lint (pass), unit tests (pass), e2e (flaky -> rerun), migration-job-integration (pass). One flaky e2e test (console profile summary snapshot) failed on first run; rerun succeeded. All checks green at merge.
Checks: [build: pass, lint: pass, unit: pass, e2e: pass after rerun, migration-integration: pass]
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash merge on 2024-11-07 by Priya Natarajan. Commit message consolidated schema + CLI + synthesizer. Post-merge: feature-flag enabled in staging same day.
source.manual_migration_steps:
1) Start migration job in 10% window (migration_job --percent=10). 2) Monitor metrics: migration_backfill_rate and release_brief_missing_ratio. 3) If error rate > 1% for 30m, pause and investigate logs at service/synthesizer. 4) Ramp to 100% over 6 windows.
Note: migration is best-effort; any failed backfills are logged to backfill_errors and can be retried per-profile.
release_notes: Add compact commit-capsule metadata with signed attachment to model profiles
Auto-generate concise release briefs from onboard capsules+perf deltas (console + downloadable capsule)
Migration tool that backfills capsules for recent profiles (90d window)
source.tests_notes: Unit tests cover schema validation, CLI emit, and synthesizer templates. Added e2e snapshot for console summary. Migration job has integration test that runs against a small profile fixture set.
source.rollback_plan: Disable synthesizer feature-flag and revert console change if brief regressions observed. Migration job can be paused; capsules are additive so rollback does not affect existing profiles. For a full revert, revert the merge commit and re-run pipeline.
source.notes_for_reviewers: Please focus on schema stability (commit_capsule_v1) and migration safety. The synthesizer templates are intentionally conservative; if you see missing critical fields we should add them to the capsule instead of complex heuristics.
source.audit_trail: Signed capsule stored with profile artifact; signing uses existing model-registry signing keyring. Migration writes an audit event per backfill (migrations.audit.backfill_created).
source.ui_changes: Profile summary now shows a one-line release brief and a 'Download capsule' button. The full brief is available in the details panel. UI change guarded by feature flag 'release_brief_ui'.
source.follow_up:
(1) ENG-1501: optional LLM-based summarizer behind feature flag.
(2) ENG-1502: improve perf_tokens shape and semantics to include sample-size and variance.
(3) DOCS-211: add capsule schema docs to model-registry onboarding guide.
source.example_capsule: {"schema_version":"commit_capsule_v1","commit_sha":"7a2c9d9","uploader":"priya@redwood.ai","dataset_tag":"wikitext-2024-11-01","perf_tokens":"latency:28ms; throughput:1200tps; embed_l2:0.012","flags":"quant=4bit; kvcache=enabled","author_note":"Initial onboard with quant eval and small calibration set"}

source.title: Orchestrate onboarding DAG and release assembler for synthesized perf profiles
source.author: Diego Morales
source.description: Motivation: onboarding several new open models exposed a gap in how we compose metadata transforms, perf fragments, and release notes into a single deterministic model version artifact. This PR introduces an onboarding DAG (graph runner) that composes validators, normalizers, profile synthesizers, and a release-assembler that produces canonical changelog/release-note entries for each model version. What changed: - new onboarding/graph package (DAG runner, node interfaces, light scheduler) - deterministic version hash: canonical serializer + version salt derived from provenance - perf profile synthesizer: merges infra benchmark fragments, manual annotations, and quantization notes into a single profile document with provenance traces - release-assembler: template-driven changelog entries + optional GitHub release draft action - CLI hooks and GitHub Action integration (onboard:run, onboard:migrate) - migration shim to translate existing model profiles to the new canonical format - docs: onboarding guide, schema reference, and migration notes Checklist: - [x] Add DAG runner and node interfaces - [x] Implement deterministic hashing and serializer - [x] Add perf profile synthesizer and provenance fields - [x] Implement release-assembler and GitHub Action - [x] Add unit+integration tests and CI jobs - [x] Add docs and migration guide - [x] Update SDK examples to consume synthesized profiles Backwards compatibility and migration: existing profile documents are converted via a one-time migration CLI (onboard:migrate) that emits a preview bundle and a dry-run mode. We keep compatibility by preserving legacy fields under a legacy_metadata namespace and by allowing consumers to opt into the new canonical version hash. Performance expectations: profile synthesis is a CPU-bound transformation that adds ~15–40ms per onboarding job in our benchmark harness (depends on profile size). Related work: ENG-4122, ENG-4198, PM-703. See inline commits and tests for microbenchmarks and graphs.
commits: a1f3b2c: add onboarding graph engine and DAG runner
b4d9f7e: add canonical serializer and deterministic version hash
c7e112a: implement perf profile synthesizer and provenance model
d2a8f90: add release-assembler + GitHub Action for release drafts
e5b77ad: CLI hooks, migration shim, and migration docs
f112c33: tests: unit and integration for DAG, hash, synthesizer
reviews: Claire Zhang (request changes): The deterministic hash looks good but we need a clear migration story for consumers that rely on model_id. Please add a stable mapping table and a non-destructive migration mode.

Diego Morales (reply): Added a migration shim (onboard:migrate) that emits a preview bundle and mapping table. The CLI defaults to dry-run; destructive write requires --apply and an audit log entry. See docs/onboarding/migration.md for details.
Marcus Liu (comment): Tests look comprehensive; can we add a perf unit that asserts the synthesize step stays under 100ms for typical profiles? I hit a 180ms case in local run.
Diego Morales (reply): Added microbenchmarks in tests/onboarding/test_synthesizer_microbench.py and tuned the merge algorithm to avoid O(n^2) behavior for tags; typical run now 25–40ms in CI harness.
ci_checks: integration-tests: flaky first run (timeout on remote benchmark runner), re-run passed
release-action: simulated run in dry mode
source.merge: merged via squash on 2024-10-22 by Marcus Liu. Commit message: 'orchestrate onboarding graph + synthesize perf profiles + release assembler'
release_notes: Introduces an onboarding DAG runner that composes validators, normalizers, and synthesizers into a deterministic model artifact.
Adds deterministic canonical serializer and version hashing to produce stable model version identifiers.
Adds a perf profile synthesizer that merges infra fragments, annotations, and quantization notes with provenance tracing.
Adds a release-assembler that emits changelog entries and integrates with GitHub Actions to draft releases.
Includes a migration CLI (onboard:migrate) with dry-run and mapping table output to ease transition for existing profiles.

source.title: chronicle-writer: compact onboarding abstracts and deterministic release zipper
source.author: Priya Raman
source.description: Context: onboarding churn and release-note noise slow cross-team reviews. Large profile uploads include verbose provenance and full sample dumps; downstream consumers only need a small, deterministic artifact to verify compatibility and pull release summaries. This PR introduces the 'chronicle-writer' pipeline which emits a compact, deterministic zip (the 'chronicle') containing: a lightweight onboarding abstract (machine + human friendly), a sampled provenance manifest (configurable seed & reservoir size), delta fingerprints for fast diffs, and a concise release index used by downstream syncers. Motivation: speed up reviews, reduce artifact size for audits, and enable deterministic release replays for regressions. What this PR changes: 1) chronicle service: new CLI and server handler to build deterministic zips from profile uploads; 2) compact-onboard-abstract: a templated, schema-backed one-page summary derived from model metadata + top-k perf tokens; 3) sampled-provenance: reservoir sampler integrated into the onboard pipeline with seed control to ensure reproducible samples; 4) zipper: deterministic packaging/ordering + SHA256 manifest and timestamping; 5) release-sync helpers: small client library to fetch and validate chronicles and apply delta patches. Compatibility notes: chronicles are opt-in at onboarding and enabled by default for curated workflows; they are additive and do not replace existing full-profile bundles. Checklist: - [x] CLI + server handlers implemented - [x] deterministic ordering + manifest hashing - [x] reservoir sampling with seed control - [x] tests for determinism under varying node counts - [x] basic docs and QuickStart snippet - [x] migration script to backfill chronicles for recent 30 days Rollout plan: merge behind feature flag chronicle.enabled=false, enable for curated bucket the next day, observe storage and review latency for 72h, then flip default for new uploads. Related work: ties to ENG-8842 (release UX) and the release zipper RFC (internal link).
commits: init chronicle writer and CLI (primitives)
add compact abstract schema + templater
integrate reservoir sampler into onboard pipeline
deterministic zipper + manifest signer
client sync helper and validation tests
docs: quickstart and migration script
fix: ordering bug with nested keys in manifest
review_comments: Jared Smith: Can we guarantee determinism across Python versions? Please add a pinned test against canonical Python 3.10 and 3.11 hashes.
Priya Raman: Added cross-version test vectors in tests/chronicle/test_determinism.py; CI runs both interpreters in the pipeline.
Linnea Ortega: Reservoir sampler looks good but please expose sampling seed in the onboarding API and document how to request same seed for backfills.
Priya Raman: Seed exposed via optional 'chronicle_seed' query param and migration script accepts override; docs updated.
Mateo Alvarez: Small nit: zipper ordering should normalize unicode keys. Consider NFC normalization.
Priya Raman: Normalization added in zipper and a test added to ensure identical manifests across normalization variants.
source.ci_summary: CI: unit (pass), integration (pass), lint (pass), e2e-chronicle (pass) - full matrix on python 3.10/3.11; flaky test rerun not required.
source.merge_outcome: Merged to main via squash on 2024-10-05 after approvals from Jared, Linnea; deploy gated behind feature flag.
source.release_notes: Adds 'chronicle' deterministic release artifacts: compact onboarding abstracts, sampled provenance manifests, and deterministic zips for faster review and audit. Opt-in via chronicle.enabled; backfill migration provided.
source.notes_for_ops: Feature flag chronicle.enabled=false at merge. Migration script registry/migrations/20241003_backfill_chronicles.py is idempotent and rate-limited; schedule backfill during low-traffic window. Monitor storage.delta and review.latency dashboards for first 72h.
source.qa_instructions: 1) Upload a test profile with chronicle.enabled=true and fixed chronicle_seed=42. 2) Run chronicle CLI to produce zip and validate SHA against expected vector in tests/chronicle/vectors/42.sha256. 3) Run client sync validate to ensure delta apply restores expected minimal state.
source.human_summary: This PR adds a compact, deterministic packaging flow ('chronicle') that produces small, verifiable release artifacts to accelerate onboarding reviews and reduce storage/transfer costs. It's additive, tested for determinism, and released behind a feature flag with a backfill tool.

source.title: Affinity-guided changelog composer and manifest affinity stamps
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.description: Motivation: make variant rollouts and capacity planning faster by surfacing runtime affinity signals on model manifests and by auto-drafting concise, priority-sorted changelog blurbs. This PR adds an opt-in 'affinity_stamp' metadata to manifests (hardware_family, os_kernel_tag, expected_latency_bucket), a small composer service that merges onboarding diffs with occupancy and sample-sufficiency heuristics to produce release snippets, and a migration/backfill tool that infers affinity from recent perf snapshots. The composer emits short human-readable blurbs and structured 'rollout_advisory' labels that downstream SRE and release automation can consume. Backwards compatibility: manifests without affinity_stamp are accepted and treated as 'affinity: unknown'. Feature-flagged rollout with dry-run and canary namespace. Linear links: ENG-10111 and ENG-9142. Related to automated release notes initiative ENG-8723 and SRE request SUP-321.
commits: 7f4c2d3: add affinity_stamp to manifest schema and validator
8b9a0f1: introduce affinity_changelog_composer service and aggregator
ad3e6b2: migration script to backfill affinity from perf-snapshots and docs
review_comments: Marco Alvarez: Please confirm backward compatibility with downstream ingestion pipelines; consider a validator flag for older agents.
Priya Natarajan: Responded - validator accepts v1 manifests and maps missing affinity_stamp to 'unknown'. Added an integration test for legacy ingestion.
Sofia Chen: The default 24h aggregation window will under-sample low-traffic variants; recommend a min_samples config and exposure in config/registry.yaml.
Priya Natarajan: Responded - added min_samples heuristic and made window configurable. Documented recommended defaults.
Liam O'Connor: Verified migration script idempotency by running dry-runs against staging; results attached in CI artifacts.
Marco Alvarez: Approved after CI green and a minor linter fix.
source.merge_outcome: merged (squash)
source.release_notes: Add manifest affinity_stamp (schema v2) that records runtime signals; introduce an affinity-guided changelog composer that drafts prioritized release blurbs and rollout advisories using occupancy and sample heuristics. Includes a backfill tool to infer affinity from perf snapshots. Feature-flagged and backward compatible.
source.testing: Unit tests for schema and composer added. Backfill dry-run against staging completed. Canary e2e validated manifest ingestion with affinity_stamp. Observed ~2% additional CPU on writer under 500rps due to light parsing.

title: Affinity-guided changelog composer and manifest affinity stamps
preview: Motivation: make variant rollouts and capacity planning faster by surfacing runtime affinity signals on model manifests and by auto-drafting concise, priority-sorted changelog blurbs. This PR adds an opt-in 'affinity_stamp' metadata to manifests (hardware_family, os_kernel_tag, expected_latency_bucket), a small composer service that merges onboarding diffs with occupancy and sample-sufficiency heuristics to produce release snippets, and a migration/backfill tool that infers affinity from recent perf snapshots. The composer emits short human-readable blurbs and structured 'rollout_advisory' labels that downstream SRE and release automation can consume. Backwards compatibility: manifests without affinity_stamp are accepted and treated as 'affinity: unknown'. Feature-flagged rollout with dry-run and canary namespace. Linear links: ENG-10111 and ENG-9142. Related to automated release notes initiative ENG-8723 and SRE request SUP-321.
